## אֵב (id: 000001000000000)

אֵב
===

אֵב (H0003 · A0004)

noun m

Vegetation

blossom, flower \= part of a plant or tree that is typically surrounded by brightly colored petals and will eventually develop into a fruit

[SNG 6:11](https://ref.ly/Song6:11)

Stage

**Collocation:** בְּאֵב

blossom \= state in which a plant or tree has developed blossoms

[JOB 8:12](https://ref.ly/Job8:12)

Fruits

fruit \= part of a plant or tree that carries the seed and is often edible

[DAN 4:9](https://ref.ly/Dan4:9) · [DAN 4:11](https://ref.ly/Dan4:11) · [DAN 4:18](https://ref.ly/Dan4:18)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Song of Songs 6:11; Job 8:12; Daniel 4:9; Daniel 4:11; Daniel 4:18


## אַב (id: 000002000000000)

אַב
===

אַב (A0002)

noun m

Relatives

father \= direct male progenitor; ► who normally provides protection, care, instruction, and discipline; ≈ is usually regarded with respect and associated with wisdom, security, and comfort

[DAN 5:2](https://ref.ly/Dan5:2) · [DAN 5:11](https://ref.ly/Dan5:11) · [DAN 5:11](https://ref.ly/Dan5:11) · [DAN 5:11](https://ref.ly/Dan5:11) · [DAN 5:13](https://ref.ly/Dan5:13) · [DAN 5:18](https://ref.ly/Dan5:18)

Relatives

forefather, ancestor \= indirect male progenitor

[EZR 4:15](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:15) · [EZR 5:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:12) · [DAN 2:23](https://ref.ly/Dan2:23)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Daniel 5:2; Daniel 5:11; Daniel 5:13; Daniel 5:18; Ezra 4:15; Ezra 5:12; Daniel 2:23

Possess

**Collocation:** אָבֹות

family possessions, patrimony \= possessions belonging to a group of people that share a common ancestry

[DEU 18:8](https://ref.ly/Deut18:8)

Titles

father (as title) literally: father; hence: \= a way of addressing a male person that one regards as one's superior because of his age, status, authority, etc.

Non\-Space

to be lost \= state of not being located in the place where it is expected to be

[DEU 22:3](https://ref.ly/Deut22:3) · [PSA 31:13](https://ref.ly/Ps31:13)1

Non\-Space

to wander, to stray \= action by which someone does not stay in a single expected location but moves about from place to place without a clear destination

Think

## אֹבֵד (id: 000006000000000)

אֹבֵד
=====

אֹבֵד (H0008)

verb · אבד

Non\-Exist · Dead

destruction \= process of disappearing or being removed from existence;◄ either by (a usually unnatural) death, aggression, hardship, disaster, natural processes, acts of destruction, or direct divine intervention

[NUM 24:20](https://ref.ly/Num24:20)1 · [NUM 24:24](https://ref.ly/Num24:24)1

verb · אבד

Endure

to be permanent \= process of having permanence through time

[NUM 24:20](https://ref.ly/Num24:20)1 · [NUM 24:24](https://ref.ly/Num24:24)1

1 NUM 24:20 · NUM 24:24 A proposal has been made of a root אבד II meaning "to endure, to be permanent" (cf. Jenni, THAT 1:18 \| Otzen, TWAT 1:21\). It is now accepted, or at least strongly suggested, by Gesenius (18\) and KAHAL. See the note in אבד 2\.

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 24:20; Numbers 24:24

## אַבִּיר (id: 000045000000000)

אַבִּיר
=======

אַבִּיר (H0047)

adj · אבר

Domestic Animals

bull \= male bovine; ≈ symbol of deities and associated with power and strength, but also with aggression and therefore often used to denote people who behave as such

[PSA 50:13](https://ref.ly/Ps50:13)

Titles · Deities

mighty (bull) \= epithet for Apis, a deity shaped like a bull, ◄ worshiped by Egyptians

[JER 46:15](https://ref.ly/Jer46:15)1

Strong

bull, mighty one \= powerful person ◄ compared with a bull; ≈ associated with power, authority, and/or aggression

[PSA 22:13](https://ref.ly/Ps22:13) · [PSA 68:31](https://ref.ly/Ps68:31) · [ISA 34:7](https://ref.ly/Isa34:7)

Domestic Animals

stallion \= male horse; ► mainly used for carrying soldiers and drawing chariots; ≈ associated with power, strength, and warfare

[JDG 5:22](https://ref.ly/Judg5:22) · [JER 8:16](https://ref.ly/Jer8:16) · [JER 47:3](https://ref.ly/Jer47:3) · [JER 50:11](https://ref.ly/Jer50:11)

Strong

mighty one \= powerful person

[JOB 24:22](https://ref.ly/Job24:22) · [JOB 34:20](https://ref.ly/Job34:20) · [ISA 10:13](https://ref.ly/Isa10:13)2 · [JER 46:15](https://ref.ly/Jer46:15)1 · [LAM 1:15](https://ref.ly/Lam1:15)

Confident

**Collocation:** אַבִּיר לֵב

strong\-willed, brave literally: mighty of heart; hence: \= person who is determined to pursue his/her goals, ≈ which can be a sign of bravery but also of stubbornness

[PSA 76:6](https://ref.ly/Ps76:6) · [ISA 46:12](https://ref.ly/Isa46:12)3

Deities

heavenly being, angel \= supernatural being

[PSA 78:25](https://ref.ly/Ps78:25)

Leaders

chief \= person who is in a position of leadership among his peers

[1SA 21:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:8)

1 JER 46:15 Some translations and commentators interpret the plural as a pluralis majestatis, a reference to the Egyptian god Apis \| this is even more likely to be true if the preceding נסחף is read as נס חף "Apis has fled". HOTTP\-B NRSV NJB NRSV REB GNT CEV \= \[1b] \| NIV "warriors" NJPS "stalwarts" \= \[1e] 2 ISA 10:13 MT כַּאבִּיר \| NJPS "vast (populations)" 3 ISA 46:12 LXX οἱ ἀπολωλεκότες τὴν καρδίαν \= אבד

Reinier de Blois

## אבל (id: 000055000000000)

אבל
===

אבל (H0056)

verb · אֵבֶל · אָבֵל

Grieve

to mourn \= to experience grief and show this by one's actions; ◄ caused by bereavement, loss, or other difficult circumstances

[GEN 37:34](https://ref.ly/Gen37:34) · [EXO 33:4](https://ref.ly/Exod33:4) · [NUM 14:39](https://ref.ly/Num14:39) · [1SA 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:19) · [1SA 15:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:35) · [1SA 16:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:1) · [2SA 13:37](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:37) · [2SA 14:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:2) · [2SA 14:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:2) · [2SA 19:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:2) · [1CH 7:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:22) · [2CH 35:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:24) · [EZR 10:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:6) · [NEH 1:4](https://ref.ly/Neh1:4) · [NEH 8:9](https://ref.ly/Neh8:9) · [JOB 14:22](https://ref.ly/Job14:22) · [ISA 3:26](https://ref.ly/Isa3:26) · [ISA 19:8](https://ref.ly/Isa19:8) · [ISA 66:10](https://ref.ly/Isa66:10) · [JER 14:2](https://ref.ly/Jer14:2) · [EZK 7:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:12) · [EZK 7:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:27) · [DAN 10:2](https://ref.ly/Dan10:2) · [HOS 10:5](https://ref.ly/Hos10:5) · [JOL 1:9](https://ref.ly/Joel1:9) · [AMO 8:8](https://ref.ly/Amos8:8) · [AMO 9:5](https://ref.ly/Amos9:5)

Dry

to mourn, to wither, to languish \= process by which plants and their products become devoid of moisture, and therefore lose color and bend over ◄ as if in mourning, ► which may ultimately result in death if circumstances do not change; ≈ associated with drought and destruction, often as punishment from God

[ISA 24:4](https://ref.ly/Isa24:4) · [ISA 24:7](https://ref.ly/Isa24:7) · [ISA 33:9](https://ref.ly/Isa33:9) · [JER 4:28](https://ref.ly/Jer4:28) · [JER 12:4](https://ref.ly/Jer12:4) · [JER 12:11](https://ref.ly/Jer12:11) · [JER 23:10](https://ref.ly/Jer23:10) · [HOS 4:3](https://ref.ly/Hos4:3) · [JOL 1:10](https://ref.ly/Joel1:10) · [AMO 1:2](https://ref.ly/Amos1:2)

Grieve

to cause to mourn \= causative action by which deities cause or allow humans to experience grief ◄ as a result of bereavement, loss, or other difficult circumstances; ≈ in all cases personified to apply to inanimate objects such as parts of nature or buildings

[LAM 2:8](https://ref.ly/Lam2:8) · [EZK 31:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:15)

12:3](https://ref.ly/Zech12:3)

Titles

**Collocation:** אֶבֶן יִשְׂרָאֵל

Stone of Israel, Rock of Israel \= God ◄ described as an unmoveable stone, ► signifying that he is dependable and able to protect against the enemy

[GEN 49:24](https://ref.ly/Gen49:24)

Titles

**Collocation:** אֶבֶן נֶגֶף

(God as a) stumbling stone \= God ◄ described as a stone over which one may stumble, ► signifying that he will punish those who do not honor him

[ISA 8:14](https://ref.ly/Isa8:14)

Dead

**Collocation:** ירד אֶל־אַבְנֵי־בֹור

to go to the stones of the pit \> to die literally: to descend to the stones of the pit; hence: \= to die and go to the world of the dead, depicted as a deep rocky pit

[ISA 14:19](https://ref.ly/Isa14:19)

Non\-Exist

**Collocation:** נטה אַבְנֵי־בֹהוּ

to stretch the plummet of chaos over (something) \> to reduce (something) to chaos literally: to stretch out the plummet of chaos over something; hence: \= to reduce a land to destruction, ◄ as if it were meticulously planned and methodologically executed with the help of a plummet

[ISA 34:11](https://ref.ly/Isa34:11)

Confident

**Collocation:** צוק כְּמֹו־אֶבֶן

to be hard as stone (of one's heart) \> to not be easily frightened literally: (one's) heart is hard as stone; hence: \= state of not being easily frightened

[JOB 41:16](https://ref.ly/Job41:16)

Constructions

stone \= block of stone, whether fully square or not;◄ found in nature or hewn from a quarry; ► used for building houses, walls, and other constructions

[GEN 11:3](https://ref.ly/Gen11:3) · [LEV 14:40](https://ref.ly/Lev14:40) · [LEV 14:42](https://ref.ly/Lev14:42) · [LEV 14:42](https://ref.ly/Lev14:42) · [LEV 14:43](https://ref.ly/Lev14:43) · [LEV 14:45](https://ref.ly/Lev14:45) · [LEV 26:1](https://ref.ly/Lev26:1) · [2SA 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:11) · [1KI 5:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:31) · [1KI 5:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:31) · [1KI 5:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:31) · [1KI 5:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:32) · [1KI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:7) · [1KI 6:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:18) · [1KI 7:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:9) · [1KI 7:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:10) · [1KI 7:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:10) · [1KI 7:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:10) · [1KI 7:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:10) · [1KI 7:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:11) · [1KI 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:22) · [2KI 3:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:25) · [2KI 12:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:13) · [2KI 12:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:13) · [2KI 16:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:17) · [2KI 22:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:6) · [1CH 22:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:2) · [1CH 22:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:14) · [1CH 22:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:15) · [2CH 16:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:6) · [2CH 34:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:11) · [EZR 5:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:8) · [EZR 6:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:4) · [NEH 3:34](https://ref.ly/Neh3:34) · [NEH 3:35](https://ref.ly/Neh3:35) · [JOB 38:6](https://ref.ly/Job38:6) · [PSA 102:15](https://ref.ly/Ps102:15) · [PSA 118:22](https://ref.ly/Ps118:22) · [PRO 24:31](https://ref.ly/Prov24:31) · [ECC 10:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:9) · [ISA 28:16](https://ref.ly/Isa28:16) · [ISA 28:16](https://ref.ly/Isa28:16) · [ISA 54:11](https://ref.ly/Isa54:11) · [ISA 60:17](https://ref.ly/Isa60:17) · [EZK 26:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:12) · [EZK 40:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:42) · [MIC 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mic1:6) · [HAB 2:11](https://ref.ly/Hab2:11) · [HAG 2:15](https://ref.ly/Hag2:15) · [HAG 2:15](https://ref.ly/Hag2:15) · [ZEC 4:7](https://ref.ly/Zech4:7) · [ZEC 5:4](https://ref.ly/Zech5:4)

Gemstones

**Collocation:** אֶבֶן · אֶבֶן יְקָרָה

precious stone \= small object ◄ consisting of a variety of stone that is considered precious because of its beauty and scarcity; ► used as ornament or decoration; ≈ association with honor, majesty, and glory

Rain

hailstone \= object similar to a small stone both in shape and in hardness; ◄ consisting of frozen rain; ► falling from the sky in showers; ≈ often associated with a punishment from God

[JOS 10:11](https://ref.ly/Josh10:11) · [JOS 10:11](https://ref.ly/Josh10:11) · [ISA 30:30](https://ref.ly/Isa30:30) · [EZK 13:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:11) · [EZK 13:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:13) · [EZK 38:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:22)

Containers

cover, lid literally: leaden stone; hence: \= a flat object used to cover a container ◄ in the same way as a stone can be used to cover a well

[ZEC 5:8](https://ref.ly/Zech5:8)

Unwilling

**Collocation:** לֵב אֶבֶן

heart of stone, unwillingness, unresponsiveness, stubbornness literally: heart of stone; hence: \= state of being unwilling to listen to advice from others, especially from God, ◄ in the same way as stone is hard and unyielding as opposed to flesh

[EZK 11:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:19) · [EZK 36:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:26)

1 ECC 3:5 · ECC 3:5 The meaning of this collocation is unsure \| among the possibilities are: (1\) to throw stones on a field to make it unsuitable for cultivation, (2\) to throw stones that were found on a field aside, (3\) to lay bricks, (4\) a euphemism for having sex, etc. 2 ZEC 3:9 · ZEC 3:9 The meaning of this passage is disputed.

## אֹבֶן (id: 000066000000000)

אֹבֶן
=====

אֹבֶן (H0070) — אָבְנַיִם

noun m · אֶבֶן

Tools

pottery wheel \= flat, horizontal wheel made of stone, with a pointed projection on the underside; this projection sat in an indentation on a stationary lower stone and served as an axis around which the upper stone turned; ► used by a potter for making pottery

[JER 18:3](https://ref.ly/Jer18:3)

Tools

birthstool \= set of two flat objects ◄ made of stone and used in Egypt ► on which a woman used to sit or kneel while giving birth

[EXO 1:16](https://ref.ly/Exod1:16)1

1 EXO 1:16 literally: "the two stones" \| this may also be a euphemism for the female genitals (see NJB).

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 18:3; Exodus 1:16

noun f · אֶבֶן · בֵּן · רְאוּבֵן

Names of Landforms

stone (of) Bohan son of Reuben \= stone or rock ◄ located on boundary between territories of Judah and Benjamin; ≈ associated with Bohan, one of the sons of Reuben

[JOS 15:6](https://ref.ly/Josh15:6) · [JOS 18:17](https://ref.ly/Josh18:17)

## אֲגֻדָּה (id: 000093000000000)

אֲגֻדָּה
========

אֲגֻדָּה (H0092)

noun f

Cords

cord \= cord ► used to fasten one object to another

[ISA 58:6](https://ref.ly/Isa58:6)

Bind

bundle \= a collection of objects ◄ fastened together with a piece of rope or cord

[EXO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Exod12:22)

Associate

group, band \= group ◄ of people ► united for a common purpose

[2SA 2:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:25)

Buildings

vault, dome \= arched structure ► functioning either as the foundation of a house, or as its upper structure

[AMO 9:6](https://ref.ly/Amos9:6)1

1 AMO 9:6 The exact referent of אַגֻדָּה here has been under discussion. According to Shalom Paul, it refers to "the tightly fitted vault of heaven otherwise called רָקִיעַ or חוּג." Andersen and Freedman analyse the meaning of the word at length, and suggest the translation "its structures", considering the suffix as referring to מַעַלֹותֹו . The image would be of God's sanctuary with one part in heaven and the other on earth. Semantically, the question is whether אַגֻדָּה means a foundation of a building or its upper part.

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 58:6; Exodus 12:22; 2 Samuel 2:25; Amos 9:6

אַדִּיר

## אדם (id: 000123000000000)

אדם
===

אדם (H0119) [SAHD](https://sahd-online.com/words/2-d-m/)

verb · אָדֹם · אָדָם · אֹדֶם · אֲדַמְדַּם · אֲדָמָה · אַדְמֹונִי · אֱדֹום · אָדָם · אַדְמָה · אֲדָמָה · אֲדָמִי הַנֶּקֶב

Color

to be red, to be ruddy \= state in which an object is red or reddish in color; ≈ often associated with beauty

[PRO 23:31](https://ref.ly/Prov23:31) · [LAM 4:7](https://ref.ly/Lam4:7)

Color

to paint red, to dye red \= causative action by which humans give an object a red color, ◄ either by painting or dying

[EXO 25:5](https://ref.ly/Exod25:5) · [EXO 26:14](https://ref.ly/Exod26:14) · [EXO 35:7](https://ref.ly/Exod35:7) · [EXO 35:23](https://ref.ly/Exod35:23) · [EXO 36:19](https://ref.ly/Exod36:19) · [EXO 39:34](https://ref.ly/Exod39:34) · [NAM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Nah2:4)

See

to be red \> to stand out \= state by which an object is clearly visible ◄ as if of a bright red color

[ISA 1:18](https://ref.ly/Isa1:18)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 23:31; Lamentations 4:7; Exodus 25:5; Exodus 26:14; Exodus 35:7; Exodus 35:23; Exodus 36:19; Exodus 39:34; Nahum 2:4; Isaiah 1:18


## אָדָם (id: 000124000000000)

אָדָם
=====

אָדָם (H0120 · H0121)

noun m · אדם

People

human, humankind, human being(s) \= human being as an individual or as a class of living creatures; sometimes explicitly subdivided between male and female; ≈ associated with mortality

name · אדם

Adam \= first man; ◄ created by God; ► husband of Eve, father of Cain, Abel, and Seth

Names of Locations

Adam \= town; ◄ located near the river Jordan near Zarethan

[JOS 3:16](https://ref.ly/Josh3:16) · [HOS 6:7](https://ref.ly/Hos6:7)4

## אֲדָמָה (id: 000128000000000)

אֲדָמָה
=======

אֲדָמָה (H0127 · H0128)

noun f · אדם

Land

ground, earth, land, clay \= the surface of the earth, containing the substance that God used to create animals and humans, and to which they return when they die; on this surface plants and trees grow, humans labor to grow crops and worship God, who sustains all life, but who can also strike it with a curse so that nothing grows again

אַדְמָה
=======

אַדְמָה (H0126)

name · אדם

Names of Locations

Admah \= town; ◄ located near Dead Sea; ► destroyed with Sodom and Gomorrah

## אֶדֶן (id: 000133000000000)

אֶדֶן
=====

אֶדֶן (H0134)

noun m

Constructions

base, socket \= heavy object with a flat bottom and, in some cases, a hole in its top; ◄ made of stone or metal; ► used as support for a pillar or a wooden frame, to keep it from falling and/or sinking into the ground

1 2SA 20:24 · 1KI 12:18 RSV NJPS NJB NRSV REB Adoram \| NIV GNT Adoniram \| CEV is inconsistent

## אַהַב (id: 000156000000000)

אַהַב
=====

אַהַב (H0158)

noun m · אהב

Good

**Collocation:** אֲהָבִים

to be lovely \= state of having a type of beauty that inspires feelings of affection in someone else

[PRO 5:19](https://ref.ly/Prov5:19)

People

**Collocation:** תנה אַהָבִים

lover, loved one, love gift exact meaning unsure; probably: \= person to which one directs one's feelings of affection, or objects given as token of one's love

[HOS 8:9](https://ref.ly/Hos8:9)

Tents

**Collocation:** אֹהֶל מֹועֵד

Tent of Meeting literally: Tent of Meeting; hence: \= construction, also referred to as the Tabernacle, or the Tent of the Testimony, ◄ made out of cloth or skins ► as the sanctuary where YHWH chose to meet with his people, before Solomon's temple was built

Tent of Testimony literally: Tent of Testimony; hence: \= construction, also referred to as the Tabernacle or the Tent of Meeting, ◄ made out of cloth or skins ► used to put the ark of the covenant with the stone tablets on which the testimony was written, before Solomon's temple was built

## אֲהָלֹות (id: 000167000000000)

אֲהָלֹות
========

אֲהָלֹות (H0174) — אָהָל, אֲהָלִים

noun c

Trees

agarwood tree, aloe \= large tree which may reach a height of 36 m.; native to south\-east Asia and northern India; ◄ Aquilaria ahallocha (agarwood); ► known for its fragrant wood; ≈ associated with fragrance and beauty

[NUM 24:6](https://ref.ly/Num24:6) · [SNG 4:14](https://ref.ly/Song4:14)

Spices

agarwood, aloes \= chips of fragrant wood, from the aloe tree (agarwood)

## אֹו (id: 000172000000000)

אֹו
===

אֹו (H0176)

conj

Connection

(either X) or (Y) \= particle linking two entities by which X and Y are alternative options that exclude each other

[GEN 24:49](https://ref.ly/Gen24:49) · [EXO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Exod19:13) · [LEV 1:14](https://ref.ly/Lev1:14) · [LEV 5:7](https://ref.ly/Lev5:7) · [LEV 5:11](https://ref.ly/Lev5:11) · [LEV 12:6](https://ref.ly/Lev12:6) · [LEV 12:8](https://ref.ly/Lev12:8) · [LEV 13:59](https://ref.ly/Lev13:59) · [LEV 14:22](https://ref.ly/Lev14:22) · [LEV 14:30](https://ref.ly/Lev14:30) · [LEV 15:14](https://ref.ly/Lev15:14) · [LEV 15:29](https://ref.ly/Lev15:29) · [NUM 6:10](https://ref.ly/Num6:10) · [NUM 11:8](https://ref.ly/Num11:8) · [JDG 18:19](https://ref.ly/Judg18:19) · [2SA 2:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:21) · [1KI 20:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:39) · [1KI 21:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:6) · [ECC 2:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:19) · [ECC 11:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:6) · [JER 40:5](https://ref.ly/Jer40:5)

## אוה (id: 000178000000000)

אוה
===

אוה (H0183 · H0184)

verb · אַוָּה · הַוָּה · מַאֲוַי · תַּאֲוָה · אוּאֵל

Desire

to crave, to desire \= state in which humans sense a physical craving for food or alcoholic drinks; this desire is associated with one's נֶפֶשׁ ≈ and can be both good and evil

[NUM 11:4](https://ref.ly/Num11:4) · [NUM 11:34](https://ref.ly/Num11:34) · [DEU 12:20](https://ref.ly/Deut12:20) · [DEU 14:26](https://ref.ly/Deut14:26) · [1SA 2:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:16) · [2SA 23:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:15) · [1CH 11:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:17) · [PSA 106:14](https://ref.ly/Ps106:14) · [PRO 13:4](https://ref.ly/Prov13:4) · [PRO 23:3](https://ref.ly/Prov23:3) · [PRO 23:6](https://ref.ly/Prov23:6) · [PRO 31:4](https://ref.ly/Prov31:4) · [PRO 31:4](https://ref.ly/Prov31:4) · [MIC 7:1](https://ref.ly/Mic7:1)

Wish

to desire, to long for, to covet \= state in which humans and deities experience a desire to possess something, to live somewhere, or to associate with someone; ◄ this desire is associated with one's נֶפֶשׁ; ≈ and can be both good and evil

[DEU 5:21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:21) · [2SA 3:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:21) · [1KI 11:37](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:37) · [PSA 132:13](https://ref.ly/Ps132:13) · [PSA 132:14](https://ref.ly/Ps132:14) · [ECC 6:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:2) · [ISA 26:9](https://ref.ly/Isa26:9)

Wish

to desire, to long \= to experience a desire to see something happen or to do something oneself; ◄ this desire is associated with one's נֶפֶשׁ ≈ and can be both good and evil

[JOB 23:13](https://ref.ly/Job23:13) · [PSA 45:12](https://ref.ly/Ps45:12) · [PRO 21:10](https://ref.ly/Prov21:10) · [PRO 21:26](https://ref.ly/Prov21:26)2 · [PRO 24:1](https://ref.ly/Prov24:1) · [JER 17:16](https://ref.ly/Jer17:16) · [AMO 5:18](https://ref.ly/Amos5:18)

verb

Know

to mark out \= to indicate the boundary of a piece of land by placing markers

## אַוָּה (id: 000179000000000)

אַוָּה
======

noun f · אוה

[NUM 23:21](https://ref.ly/Num23:21)1 · [JOB 5:6](https://ref.ly/Job5:6) · [JOB 18:12](https://ref.ly/Job18:12)5 · [JOB 21:19](https://ref.ly/Job21:19) · [PSA 55:4](https://ref.ly/Ps55:4) · [PSA 90:10](https://ref.ly/Ps90:10) · [PRO 12:21](https://ref.ly/Prov12:21) · [PRO 22:8](https://ref.ly/Prov22:8) · [JER 4:15](https://ref.ly/Jer4:15) · [HAB 3:7](https://ref.ly/Hab3:7)

Non\-Exist

nothing \= state of being non\-existent, implying that an existing object has been destroyed, or that an object exists in people's imagination only; ≈ regarded therefore as totally insignificant

[ISA 41:29](https://ref.ly/Isa41:29) · [ISA 66:3](https://ref.ly/Isa66:3) · [AMO 5:5](https://ref.ly/Amos5:5) · [ZEC 10:2](https://ref.ly/Zech10:2)

Names of Locations

Aven \= town and cult centre, normally referred to as Bethel or Beth\-Aven; ◄ territory of Ephraim, near the border of Benjamin; ≈ probably a pun featuring the meaning of אָוֶן "evil, iniquity" because of the idolatry that was practiced in this town

[EZK 30:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:17)6 · [HOS 10:8](https://ref.ly/Hos10:8)4

## אֵזֹוב (id: 000218000000000)

אֵזֹוב
======

אֵזֹוב (H0231)

noun m

Plants

hyssop \= plant with a shrub\-like base and stems which are erect, stiff and strong; it reaches a height of just under one meter; it may grow from the ground but also spring out of crevices in walls, cliffs and rocks; its leaves and branches are hairy, and its flowers white; ◄ origanum maru; ► the plant is aromatic, and the dried leaves are used as a condiments; the leaves and little branches, when collected in bunches, are suitable for sprinkling purposes, as the hairy surface holds the liquid; used in the ritual purification of the Jews

## אזר (id: 000232000000000)

אזר
===

אזר (H0247)

verb · אֵזֹור

Dress

to be girded, to wear \= to wear a belt or girdle around one's waist

[2KI 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:8) · [PSA 65:7](https://ref.ly/Ps65:7)

Dress

to gird oneself, to put on \= to tie a belt or girdle around one's waist; ≈ often used metaphorically to denote arming oneself for battle, or, in a more abstract sense, to "put on" certain characteristics and qualities, such as strength, courage, and aggression

[1SA 2:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:4) · [JOB 38:3](https://ref.ly/Job38:3) · [JOB 40:7](https://ref.ly/Job40:7) · [PSA 93:1](https://ref.ly/Ps93:1) · [ISA 8:9](https://ref.ly/Isa8:9) · [ISA 8:9](https://ref.ly/Isa8:9) · [ISA 50:11](https://ref.ly/Isa50:11)1 · [ISA 50:11](https://ref.ly/Isa50:11)1 · [JER 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jer1:17)

Dress

to gird (someone) \= to tie a belt or girdle around someone else's waist; ≈ often used metaphorically to denote arming someone for battle, or, in a more abstract sense, to "clothe" people with certain characteristics and qualities, such as joy, strength, and courage

[2SA 22:40](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:40) · [PSA 18:33](https://ref.ly/Ps18:33) · [PSA 18:40](https://ref.ly/Ps18:40) · [PSA 30:12](https://ref.ly/Ps30:12) · [ISA 45:5](https://ref.ly/Isa45:5)

Intense

to be tight (around someone), to choke meaning unsure; possible: \= to be tightly tied around part of one's body in such a way that it becomes a hindrance; ≈ used metaphorically to denote how suffering can be a hindrance to someone

[JOB 30:18](https://ref.ly/Job30:18)

1 ISA 50:11 MT HOTTP\-B NIV NJPS NJB \| RSV NJB NRSV REB מאירי "who set alight"

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Kings 1:8; Psalms 65:7; 1 Samuel 2:4; Job 38:3; Job 40:7; Psalms 93:1; Isaiah 8:9; Isaiah 50:11; Jeremiah 1:17; 2 Samuel 22:40; Psalms 18:33; Psalms 18:40; Psalms 30:12; Isaiah 45:5; Job 30:18

אֶזְרֹועַ (H0248) — אֶזְרֹעַ

noun f · זְרֹועַ

arm, forearm \= each of the two upper limbs of the human body from the shoulder to the hand; ≈ associated with action and strength

[JOB 31:22](https://ref.ly/Job31:22)

Strong

**Collocation:** אֶזְרֹועַ נְטוּיָה

outstretched arm, great power literally: outstretched arm; hence: \= action by which deities show their great power

[JER 32:21](https://ref.ly/Jer32:21)

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Job 31:22; Jeremiah 32:21

noun m · זרח

Classes

native\-born, citizen \= person born in the land, as opposed to the resident alien

[EXO 12:19](https://ref.ly/Exod12:19) · [EXO 12:48](https://ref.ly/Exod12:48) · [EXO 12:49](https://ref.ly/Exod12:49) · [LEV 16:29](https://ref.ly/Lev16:29) · [LEV 17:15](https://ref.ly/Lev17:15) · [LEV 18:26](https://ref.ly/Lev18:26) · [LEV 19:34](https://ref.ly/Lev19:34) · [LEV 23:42](https://ref.ly/Lev23:42)1 · [LEV 24:16](https://ref.ly/Lev24:16) · [LEV 24:22](https://ref.ly/Lev24:22) · [NUM 9:14](https://ref.ly/Num9:14) · [NUM 15:13](https://ref.ly/Num15:13) · [NUM 15:29](https://ref.ly/Num15:29) · [NUM 15:30](https://ref.ly/Num15:30) · [JOS 8:33](https://ref.ly/Josh8:33) · [EZK 47:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:22)

Trees

native tree \= tree that grows in its natural habitat; ≈ associated with well\-being

[PSA 37:35](https://ref.ly/Ps37:35)2

1 LEV 23:42 This is the only occurrence in the MT (except PSA 37:35\) in which אֶזְרָח is not paralleled by גֵר \| for an explanation, see Milgrom 2 PSA 37:35 MT HOTTP\-B אֶזְרָח רַעְַנָן NIV "a green tree in its native soil" NJPS "a robust native tree" REB "a spreading tree in its native soil" CEV strong like trees in rich soil" \| RSV NJB NRSV GNT "a cedar of Lebanon" \= ארז הלבנון

Enio R. Mueller

Relatives

brother, cousin, nephew, kinsman, relative \= close relative, such as one's uncle's son and one's brother's son, or someone else regarded as closely related

Friends

brother, friend, companion, colleague \= fellow member of the same group of people, not necessarily relatives, sharing a bond of affection, employment, or a common purpose

Titles

brother, friend only used in vocatives: \= friendly and endearing way to address a man

Object Referents

**Collocation:** אִישׁ ... אָח

a man ... his brother \> one ... each other \= expression denoting a situation in which two people or objects that are regarded as masculine are involved in a reciprocal event

## אָחֹור (id: 000251000000000)

אָחֹור
======

אָחֹור (H0268)

noun m · אחר

Spatial Referents

back \= a referent pointing to the space behind a person or object

[EXO 26:12](https://ref.ly/Exod26:12) · [EXO 33:23](https://ref.ly/Exod33:23) · [1KI 7:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:25) · [2CH 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:4) · [EZK 2:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:10) · [EZK 8:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:16)

Spatial Referents

back, backwards \= a referent pointing to the space behind a person or object; ≈ since the way the ancient Israelites oriented themselves was facing the East, the term is also often used to denote the West; also associated with failure, unfaithfulness, and outright rebellion

[GEN 49:17](https://ref.ly/Gen49:17) · [2SA 1:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:22) · [2SA 10:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:9) · [1CH 19:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:10) · [2CH 13:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:14) · [JOB 23:8](https://ref.ly/Job23:8) · [PSA 9:4](https://ref.ly/Ps9:4) · [PSA 35:4](https://ref.ly/Ps35:4) · [PSA 40:15](https://ref.ly/Ps40:15) · [PSA 44:11](https://ref.ly/Ps44:11) · [PSA 44:19](https://ref.ly/Ps44:19) · [PSA 56:10](https://ref.ly/Ps56:10) · [PSA 70:3](https://ref.ly/Ps70:3) · [PSA 78:66](https://ref.ly/Ps78:66) · [PSA 114:3](https://ref.ly/Ps114:3) · [PSA 114:5](https://ref.ly/Ps114:5) · [PSA 129:5](https://ref.ly/Ps129:5) · [PSA 139:5](https://ref.ly/Ps139:5) · [PRO 29:11](https://ref.ly/Prov29:11) · [ISA 1:4](https://ref.ly/Isa1:4) · [ISA 9:11](https://ref.ly/Isa9:11) · [ISA 28:13](https://ref.ly/Isa28:13) · [ISA 42:17](https://ref.ly/Isa42:17) · [ISA 44:25](https://ref.ly/Isa44:25) · [ISA 50:5](https://ref.ly/Isa50:5) · [ISA 59:14](https://ref.ly/Isa59:14) · [JER 7:24](https://ref.ly/Jer7:24) · [JER 15:6](https://ref.ly/Jer15:6) · [JER 38:22](https://ref.ly/Jer38:22) · [JER 46:5](https://ref.ly/Jer46:5) · [LAM 1:8](https://ref.ly/Lam1:8) · [LAM 1:13](https://ref.ly/Lam1:13) · [LAM 2:3](https://ref.ly/Lam2:3)

Time

**Collocation:** לְאָחֹור

hereafter \= referent to a time that is yet to come

[ISA 41:23](https://ref.ly/Isa41:23) · [ISA 42:23](https://ref.ly/Isa42:23)

verb · אָחוּז · אֲחֻזָּה · אָחָז · אֲחַזְיָה · יְהֹואָחָז

Hold

to be held, to be caught, to be fastened, to be attached, to be girded \= to be held in position firmly in a certain place

[GEN 22:13](https://ref.ly/Gen22:13) · [1KI 6:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:6) · [1KI 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:10) · [2CH 9:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:18) · [EST 1:6](https://ref.ly/Esth1:6)

Hold

to grasp, to take hold of (something), to seize, to catch, to hold on (to something) \= to take hold of someone or something; humans typically do this with their hands and animals use their mouths; however, it is often extended to include inanimate objects (traps), personalized qualities (wisdom), emotions (anger, fear), or processes (time)

Hold

to bolt (a door) \= to cause a door to be kept firmly locked by securing it with an iron bar

[NEH 7:3](https://ref.ly/Neh7:3)

Engage

to hold on (to a type of behavior) literally: to hold on to a quality as if it were a person; hence: \= to act in accordance with a certain quality

Dissociate

Cover

Wise

skilled swordsman literally: held by the sword; hence: \= state in which warriors carry swords and have the skill to use it

[JDG 5:28](https://ref.ly/Judg5:28) · [ISA 46:13](https://ref.ly/Isa46:13) · [HAB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Hab2:3)

Delay

[GEN 32:5](https://ref.ly/Gen32:5) · [GEN 34:19](https://ref.ly/Gen34:19) · [DEU 7:10](https://ref.ly/Deut7:10) · [DEU 23:22](https://ref.ly/Deut23:22) · [2SA 20:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:5)1 · [PSA 40:18](https://ref.ly/Ps40:18) · [PSA 70:6](https://ref.ly/Ps70:6) · [PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2) · [PRO 23:30](https://ref.ly/Prov23:30) · [ECC 5:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:3) · [ISA 5:11](https://ref.ly/Isa5:11) · [DAN 9:19](https://ref.ly/Dan9:19)

אַחַר (H0310 · A0311) — אַחֲרֵי

noun m · אחר

Spatial Referents

behind \= a referent pointing to the space behind a person or object

Faithful

behind, after, following literally: behind; hence: \= a state in which humans are committed to follow the leadership and example of other humans or deities

Great

after literally: behind (in rank); hence: \= state in which humans have a lesser status than other humans

back, butt literally: behind; hence: \= the side or part of something away from the spectator or from the direction in which it moves or faces

after, since, as soon as \= a referent point to the time following a certain event

## אַחֲרֹון (id: 000295000000000)

אַחֲרֹון
========

אַחֲרֹון (H0314)

adj · אחר

Spatial Referents

behind, last \= a referent pointing to the space behind a person or object

[GEN 33:2](https://ref.ly/Gen33:2) · [GEN 33:2](https://ref.ly/Gen33:2) · [JOB 18:20](https://ref.ly/Job18:20)1

Names of Waterbodies

**Collocation:** הַיָּם הָאַחֲרֹון

Mediterranean, Western Sea literally: the sea behind; hence: \= Mediterranean Sea

[DEU 11:24](https://ref.ly/Deut11:24) · [DEU 34:2](https://ref.ly/Deut34:2) · [JOL 2:20](https://ref.ly/Joel2:20) · [ZEC 14:8](https://ref.ly/Zech14:8)

Circumstantial Referents

latter, last, next, ... to come, future \= a referent pointing to the last in a range of events

[EXO 4:8](https://ref.ly/Exod4:8) · [NUM 2:31](https://ref.ly/Num2:31) · [DEU 13:10](https://ref.ly/Deut13:10) · [DEU 17:7](https://ref.ly/Deut17:7) · [DEU 24:3](https://ref.ly/Deut24:3) · [DEU 24:3](https://ref.ly/Deut24:3) · [DEU 29:21](https://ref.ly/Deut29:21) · [RUT 3:10](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:10) · [1SA 29:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam29:2) · [2SA 2:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:26) · [2SA 19:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:12) · [2SA 19:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:13) · [2SA 23:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:1) · [1KI 17:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:13) · [1CH 23:27](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:27) · [1CH 29:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:29) · [2CH 9:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:29) · [2CH 12:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:15) · [2CH 16:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:11) · [2CH 20:34](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:34) · [2CH 25:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:26) · [2CH 26:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:22) · [2CH 28:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:26) · [2CH 35:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:27) · [EZR 8:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:13) · [NEH 8:18](https://ref.ly/Neh8:18) · [JOB 19:25](https://ref.ly/Job19:25) · [PSA 48:14](https://ref.ly/Ps48:14) · [PSA 78:4](https://ref.ly/Ps78:4) · [PSA 78:6](https://ref.ly/Ps78:6) · [PSA 102:19](https://ref.ly/Ps102:19) · [PRO 31:25](https://ref.ly/Prov31:25) · [ECC 1:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:11) · [ECC 1:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:11) · [ECC 4:16](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:16) · [ISA 8:23](https://ref.ly/Isa8:23) · [ISA 30:8](https://ref.ly/Isa30:8) · [ISA 41:4](https://ref.ly/Isa41:4) · [ISA 44:6](https://ref.ly/Isa44:6) · [ISA 48:12](https://ref.ly/Isa48:12) · [JER 50:17](https://ref.ly/Jer50:17) · [DAN 8:3](https://ref.ly/Dan8:3) · [DAN 11:29](https://ref.ly/Dan11:29) · [HAG 2:9](https://ref.ly/Hag2:9)

## אַחֲרִית (id: 000301000000000)

אַחֲרִית
========

אַחֲרִית (H0319)

noun f · אחר

Spatial Referents

farthest \= a referent pointing to the space far behind a person or object; ≈ since the way the ancient Israelites oriented themselves was with their backs facing the East, this term presupposes a westerly direction

[PSA 139:9](https://ref.ly/Ps139:9)

Alive

last, surviving literally: last (ones); hence: \= state in which humans are still alive after many others have died

End

end, outcome, future \= pertaining to being the last part of an event, close to its moment of completion

## אֲחֹרַנִּית (id: 000303000000000)

אֲחֹרַנִּית
===========

אֲחֹרַנִּית (H0322)

adv · אחר

Spatial Referents

backwards \= pertaining to a movement in a direction away from the spectator or from the direction in which it moves or faces

[GEN 9:23](https://ref.ly/Gen9:23) · [GEN 9:23](https://ref.ly/Gen9:23) · [1SA 4:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:18) · [2KI 20:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:10) · [2KI 20:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:11) · [ISA 38:8](https://ref.ly/Isa38:8)

Think

**Collocation:** סבב לֵב אֲחֹרַנִּית

(God) turns someone's heart (\> allegiance) back (to himself) literally: to turn one's heart backwards; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause humans to refocus their thinking on the things on which it had been focused before it was diverted in a different direction

[1KI 18:37](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:37)

## אָטָד (id: 000308000000000)

אָטָד
=====

אָטָד (H0329) — הָאָטָד

noun m

Plants

boxthorn, bramble, thorn, thornbush \= a tree that grows to 5 meters (17 feet), has small leaves forming an oval crown, and very sharp thorns; Lycaeum europaeum (HALOT, DCH, NIDB 1:496\); or possibly a thorny bush of the Rubus genus, which includes several hundred species (ABD 2:80\)

[JDG 9:14](https://ref.ly/Judg9:14) · [JDG 9:15](https://ref.ly/Judg9:15) · [JDG 9:15](https://ref.ly/Judg9:15) · [PSA 58:10](https://ref.ly/Ps58:10)1

noun m

1 PSA 58:10 This verse is difficult to translate (see Dahood's comment). NJPS "Before the thorns grow into a bramble" \| REB "may they be rooted up like a thorn bush" count with different contextual images than Fire.

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 9:14; Judges 9:15; Psalms 58:10

## אֵי (id: 000316000000000)

אֵי
===

אֵי (H0335)

inter

Spatial Referents

where? \= referent to an unknown location; ► normally used with the purpose of finding which location that is; sometimes used in rhetorical questions expressing one's conviction that such a location cannot be found

[GEN 3:9](https://ref.ly/Gen3:9) · [GEN 4:9](https://ref.ly/Gen4:9) · [EXO 2:20](https://ref.ly/Exod2:20) · [DEU 32:37](https://ref.ly/Deut32:37) · [1SA 26:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:16) · [2KI 19:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:13) · [JOB 14:10](https://ref.ly/Job14:10) · [JOB 20:7](https://ref.ly/Job20:7) · [ISA 19:12](https://ref.ly/Isa19:12) · [MIC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Mic7:10) · [NAM 3:17](https://ref.ly/Nah3:17)

Spatial Referents

**Collocation:** אֵי זֶה

where, which \= referent to an unknown or not yet identified location; ► normally used with the purpose of finding which location that is; sometimes used in rhetorical questions expressing one's conviction that such a location cannot be found

[1SA 9:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:18) · [1KI 13:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:12) · [1KI 22:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:24)1 · [2KI 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:8) · [2CH 18:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:23) · [EST 7:5](https://ref.ly/Esth7:5) · [JOB 28:12](https://ref.ly/Job28:12) · [JOB 28:20](https://ref.ly/Job28:20) · [JOB 38:19](https://ref.ly/Job38:19) · [JOB 38:19](https://ref.ly/Job38:19) · [JOB 38:24](https://ref.ly/Job38:24) · [ECC 2:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:3) · [ECC 11:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:6) · [ISA 50:1](https://ref.ly/Isa50:1) · [ISA 66:1](https://ref.ly/Isa66:1) · [ISA 66:1](https://ref.ly/Isa66:1) · [JER 6:16](https://ref.ly/Jer6:16)

Spatial Referents

**Collocation:** אֵי מִזֶּה

from where? \= referent to an unknown location; ► normally used with the purpose of finding out from which location a person or object originates; sometimes used in rhetorical questions expressing one's conviction that that location does not exist

[GEN 16:8](https://ref.ly/Gen16:8) · [JDG 13:6](https://ref.ly/Judg13:6) · [1SA 25:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:11) · [1SA 30:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:13) · [2SA 1:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:3) · [2SA 1:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:13) · [2SA 15:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:2) · [JOB 2:2](https://ref.ly/Job2:2) · [JON 1:8](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:8)

Perceptive Referents

**Collocation:** אֵי לָזֹאת

why, how \= referent to an unknown event; ► which should make the main event in focus possible; sometimes used in rhetorical questions expressing one's conviction that that event cannot possibly take place

[JER 5:7](https://ref.ly/Jer5:7)

## אַיֵּה (id: 000320000000000)

אַיֵּה
======

אַיֵּה (H0346)

inter

Spatial Referents

where? \= a reference to an unknown location; normally used with the purpose of finding out which location that is; sometimes used in rhetorical questions expressing one's conviction that such a location cannot be found

[GEN 18:9](https://ref.ly/Gen18:9) · [GEN 19:5](https://ref.ly/Gen19:5) · [GEN 22:7](https://ref.ly/Gen22:7) · [GEN 38:21](https://ref.ly/Gen38:21) · [JDG 9:38](https://ref.ly/Judg9:38) · [2SA 16:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:3) · [2SA 17:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:20)

Spatial Referents

where? \= a reference to an unknown location, but focused on the question whether an object actually exists at all, rather than with a specific location in mind

[2KI 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:14) · [2KI 18:34](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:34) · [2KI 18:34](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:34) · [JOB 15:23](https://ref.ly/Job15:23) · [JOB 21:28](https://ref.ly/Job21:28) · [JOB 21:28](https://ref.ly/Job21:28) · [JOB 35:10](https://ref.ly/Job35:10) · [PSA 42:4](https://ref.ly/Ps42:4) · [PSA 42:11](https://ref.ly/Ps42:11) · [PSA 79:10](https://ref.ly/Ps79:10) · [PSA 115:2](https://ref.ly/Ps115:2) · [ISA 33:18](https://ref.ly/Isa33:18) · [ISA 33:18](https://ref.ly/Isa33:18) · [ISA 33:18](https://ref.ly/Isa33:18) · [ISA 36:19](https://ref.ly/Isa36:19) · [ISA 36:19](https://ref.ly/Isa36:19) · [ISA 37:13](https://ref.ly/Isa37:13) · [ISA 63:11](https://ref.ly/Isa63:11) · [ISA 63:11](https://ref.ly/Isa63:11) · [JER 2:6](https://ref.ly/Jer2:6) · [JER 2:8](https://ref.ly/Jer2:8) · [JER 2:28](https://ref.ly/Jer2:28) · [JER 13:20](https://ref.ly/Jer13:20) · [JER 37:19](https://ref.ly/Jer37:19) · [LAM 2:12](https://ref.ly/Lam2:12) · [EZK 13:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:12) · [HOS 13:10](https://ref.ly/Hos13:10)2 · [JOL 2:17](https://ref.ly/Joel2:17) · [NAM 2:12](https://ref.ly/Nah2:12) · [ZEC 1:5](https://ref.ly/Zech1:5) · [MAL 2:17](https://ref.ly/Mal2:17)

Circumstantial Referents

where? \= a reference to an unknown location, but focused on the (often rhetorical) question whether an event may ever take place at all, rather than with a specific location in mind

אֵיךְ

אֵיךְ (H0349a)

inter · אֵיכָה · אֵיכֹה · אֵיכָכָה · הֵיךְ

Descriptive Referents

how? \= referent to the way in which an event is performed; ► normally with the intention of finding out how, but often used in rhetorical questions in order to express one's conviction that that event in question cannot be performed at all (under the given circumstances)

noun m

Buildings

side wall, entrance, jamb \= a relatively short side wall projecting from the main wall of a building, either to create side chambers or to hold a door frame; alternatively this could be a pillar that stands flush with the wall or with the sides of an entrance way; it could also be the verticle part of a door frame

[1KI 6:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:31) · [EZK 40:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:9) · [EZK 40:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:10) · [EZK 40:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:14)4 · [EZK 40:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:14) · [EZK 40:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:16) · [EZK 40:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:16) · [EZK 40:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:21) · [EZK 40:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:24) · [EZK 40:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:26) · [EZK 40:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:29) · [EZK 40:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:31) · [EZK 40:33](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:33) · [EZK 40:34](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:34) · [EZK 40:36](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:36) · [EZK 40:37](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:37) · [EZK 40:37](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:37) · [EZK 40:38](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:38)7 · [EZK 40:48](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:48) · [EZK 40:49](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:49) · [EZK 41:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:1)8 · [EZK 41:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:3)

noun m

Trees

terebinth \= tree, more commonly known as אֵלָה, with a broad crown, heavy branches, and thick trunk; may reach a height of 7\.5 to 9 m.; sheds its leaves; has small an inconspicuous flowers; ◄ pictacia terebinthus; ► its stem contains aromatic turpentine, a resinous juice, which flows out when the bark is cut; it provides pleasant shade; ≈ imposing tree, preferred place to sit under and enjoy the shade, associated with cultic worship

[ISA 1:29](https://ref.ly/Isa1:29) · [ISA 57:5](https://ref.ly/Isa57:5) · [ISA 61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:3) · [EZK 31:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:14)

## אַיָּל (id: 000331000000000)

אַיָּל
======

אַיָּל (H0354)

noun m · אַיָּלָה · אַיֶּלֶת

Wild Animals

fallow deer \= wild animal, standing about a meter high, that ruminates, and of which the male grows antlers, which are dropped every year; has a reddish fur with white spots during summer; during winter the white spots disappear and the coat becomes darker; ◄ belongs to family of cervidae; ► clean animal and can therefore be eaten; ≈ associated with strength, speed, and agility; the male is also associated with sexual prowess

[DEU 12:15](https://ref.ly/Deut12:15) · [DEU 12:22](https://ref.ly/Deut12:22) · [DEU 14:5](https://ref.ly/Deut14:5) · [DEU 15:22](https://ref.ly/Deut15:22) · [1KI 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:3) · [PSA 42:2](https://ref.ly/Ps42:2) · [SNG 2:9](https://ref.ly/Song2:9) · [SNG 2:17](https://ref.ly/Song2:17) · [SNG 8:14](https://ref.ly/Song8:14) · [ISA 35:6](https://ref.ly/Isa35:6) · [LAM 1:6](https://ref.ly/Lam1:6)

Reinier de Blois

## אִין (id: 000348000000000)

אִין
====

אִין (H0371)

conj

Exist

**Collocation:** אִין יֵשׁ

is there ...? meaning unsure; probably: \= a polite inquiry as to whether an object is present

[1SA 21:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:9)1

1 1SA 21:9 LXX הֵן הֲיֵשׁ "see, is there ...?" \| DCH אַיִן יֵשׁ \| HALOT אֵי יֵשׁ or אֵין יֵשׁ

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 21:9


## אַיִן (id: 000349000000000)

אַיִן
=====

אַיִן (H0369 · H0370)

noun m

inter

Spatial Referents

**Collocation:** מֵאַיִן

from where? \= referent to an unknown location; ► normally used with the purpose of finding out from which location a person or object originates; ≈ sometimes used in rhetorical questions expressing one's conviction that that location does not exist

[GEN 29:4](https://ref.ly/Gen29:4) · [GEN 42:7](https://ref.ly/Gen42:7) · [NUM 11:13](https://ref.ly/Num11:13) · [JOS 2:4](https://ref.ly/Josh2:4) · [JOS 9:8](https://ref.ly/Josh9:8) · [JDG 17:9](https://ref.ly/Judg17:9) · [JDG 19:17](https://ref.ly/Judg19:17) · [2KI 5:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:25)4 · [2KI 6:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:27) · [2KI 20:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:14) · [JOB 1:7](https://ref.ly/Job1:7) · [JOB 28:12](https://ref.ly/Job28:12) · [JOB 28:20](https://ref.ly/Job28:20) · [PSA 121:1](https://ref.ly/Ps121:1) · [ISA 39:3](https://ref.ly/Isa39:3) · [JON 1:8](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:8) · [NAM 3:7](https://ref.ly/Nah3:7)

Old

**Collocation:** בוא בַּאֲנָשִׁים

to be advanced in years literally: to come among people; hence, probably: \= state in which people have reached an advanced age, meaning, among other things, that they are too old for military duty

[1SA 17:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:12)4

Priests

**Collocation:** אִישׁ נֹשֵׂא אֵפֹוד בָּד

person wearing a linen apron literally: a person wearing a linen ephod; hence: \= an adult member of a priestly family ◄ who are easily recognizable because of the white linen apron that they are wearing

[1SA 22:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:18)

Prophets

**Collocation:** אִישׁ אֱלֹהִים

man of God literally: person of God; hence: \= a person who is in a special relationship with God and has prophetic gifts

Prophets

**Collocation:** אִישׁ רוּחַ

inspired man literally: person of spirit; hence: \= a person endowed with the Spirit of God and has prophetic gifts

[HOS 9:7](https://ref.ly/Hos9:7)

Soldiers

**Collocation:** אִישׁ בֵּנַיִם

champion literally: person of in between; hence: \= a soldier who fights in single combat on behalf of the entire army

[1SA 17:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:4) · [1SA 17:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:8) · [1SA 17:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:10) · [1SA 17:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:23)

Soldiers

**Collocation:** אִישׁ מִלְחָמָה

warrior literally: person of battle; \= person of military age, trained and experienced in warfare; ≈ associated with strength and bravery

swordsman literally: person drawing the sword; hence: \= soldier armed with a sword

1 1KI 10:8 · 2CH 9:7 NEW NOTE 2 ECC 12:3 possibly an allusion to a person's arms or legs 3 EZK 24:17 · EZK 24:22 MT לֶחֶם אֲנָשׁים LXX "ἄρτον ἀνδρῶν" NJPS "bread of comforters" NJB "the usual food" ESV "bread of men" \| VUL "cibos lugentium" RSV NRSV "bread of mourners" NIV "food of mourners" REB "bread of sorrow" GNT CEV "food that mourners eat" \= לֶחֶם אֹונִים 4 1SA 17:12 Some suggest to read אְַנָשִׁים as שָׁנִים "years", but this is not strictly necessary in order to become to the same interpretation.

Reinier de Blois  
 David E.S. Stein

## אִישׁ בֹּשֶׁת (id: 000355000000000)

אִישׁ בֹּשֶׁת
=============

אִישׁ בֹּשֶׁת (H0378) — אִישׁ־בֹּשֶׁת

name

Names of People

Ish\-Bosheth \= man, also known as Ishvi, whose real name probably was Eshbaal, but which was changed in order to remove any reference to Baal; ◄ son of king Saul; tribe of Benjamin; ► succeeded his father as king of the northern tribes until he was assassinated and David took his place

[2SA 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:8) · [2SA 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:10) · [2SA 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:12) · [2SA 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:15) · [2SA 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:8) · [2SA 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:14) · [2SA 3:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:15) · [2SA 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:5) · [2SA 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:8) · [2SA 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:8) · [2SA 4:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:12)

Reinier de Blois

## אִישֹׁון (id: 000357000000000)

אִישֹׁון
========

אִישֹׁון (H0380a)

noun m

**Collocation:** אִישֹׁון עַיִן · אִישֹׁון בַּת־עַיִן

apple of the eye, pupil \= center part of iris of eye, ≈ associated with care as this is a very sensitive part of the body that people are inclined to take proper care of

[DEU 32:10](https://ref.ly/Deut32:10) · [PSA 17:8](https://ref.ly/Ps17:8) · [PRO 7:2](https://ref.ly/Prov7:2)

Temporal Referents

**Collocation:** אִישֹׁון חֹשֶׁךְ · אִישֹׁון לַיְלָה

utter darkness, middle of the night, dead of night literally: the center of night, darkness: \= reference to the middle of the night when the darkness is deepest

[PRO 7:9](https://ref.ly/Prov7:9)1

1 PRO 7:9 MT NJPS NJB GNT CEV \| RSV NIV GNT CEV אֱשׁוּן 2 PRO 20:20 MT\-K RSV NIV NJB NRSV REB \| MT\-Q NJPS GNT CEV אֱשׁוּן

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 32:10; Psalms 17:8; Proverbs 7:2; Proverbs 7:9

## אֵיתָן (id: 000362000000000)

אֵיתָן
======

אֵיתָן (H0386 · H0387 · H0388) — אֵתַנִים

adj

Endure

permanent, constant, perennial, enduring \= state of not being subject to change

[NUM 24:21](https://ref.ly/Num24:21) · [DEU 21:4](https://ref.ly/Deut21:4) · [JOB 12:19](https://ref.ly/Job12:19)1 · [JOB 33:19](https://ref.ly/Job33:19) · [PSA 74:15](https://ref.ly/Ps74:15) · [PRO 13:15](https://ref.ly/Prov13:15) · [JER 5:15](https://ref.ly/Jer5:15) · [JER 49:19](https://ref.ly/Jer49:19) · [JER 50:44](https://ref.ly/Jer50:44) · [AMO 5:24](https://ref.ly/Amos5:24) · [MIC 6:2](https://ref.ly/Mic6:2)

Endure

normal state \= state in which an object normally occurs

[GEN 49:24](https://ref.ly/Gen49:24) · [EXO 14:27](https://ref.ly/Exod14:27)

name

Names of Times

Ethanim \= month, equivalent to period September/October, in which only the everflowing rivers have water; ► pre\-exilic

Restrictors

only, just, mere \= particle marking an element as representing a limited number of entities

[GEN 7:23](https://ref.ly/Gen7:23) · [GEN 9:4](https://ref.ly/Gen9:4) · [GEN 9:5](https://ref.ly/Gen9:5) · [GEN 18:32](https://ref.ly/Gen18:32) · [GEN 34:15](https://ref.ly/Gen34:15) · [GEN 34:22](https://ref.ly/Gen34:22) · [EXO 10:17](https://ref.ly/Exod10:17) · [EXO 12:16](https://ref.ly/Exod12:16) · [LEV 11:4](https://ref.ly/Lev11:4) · [LEV 11:21](https://ref.ly/Lev11:21) · [LEV 11:36](https://ref.ly/Lev11:36) · [LEV 21:23](https://ref.ly/Lev21:23) · [LEV 27:26](https://ref.ly/Lev27:26) · [LEV 27:28](https://ref.ly/Lev27:28) · [NUM 1:49](https://ref.ly/Num1:49) · [NUM 12:2](https://ref.ly/Num12:2) · [NUM 18:3](https://ref.ly/Num18:3) · [NUM 18:15](https://ref.ly/Num18:15) · [NUM 18:17](https://ref.ly/Num18:17) · [NUM 22:20](https://ref.ly/Num22:20) · [NUM 31:22](https://ref.ly/Num31:22) · [NUM 36:6](https://ref.ly/Num36:6) · [DEU 14:7](https://ref.ly/Deut14:7) · [JDG 6:39](https://ref.ly/Judg6:39) · [JDG 16:28](https://ref.ly/Judg16:28) · [1SA 18:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:8) · [1SA 20:39](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:39) · [1SA 21:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:5) · [2SA 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:10) · [2SA 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:13) · [2SA 23:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:10) · [1KI 11:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:12) · [1KI 11:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:39) · [1KI 22:44](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:44) · [2CH 20:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:33) · [2CH 30:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:11) · [EZR 10:15](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:15) · [JOB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Job13:15)1 · [JOB 13:20](https://ref.ly/Job13:20) · [JOB 14:22](https://ref.ly/Job14:22) · [PSA 62:2](https://ref.ly/Ps62:2) · [PSA 62:3](https://ref.ly/Ps62:3) · [PSA 62:6](https://ref.ly/Ps62:6) · [PSA 62:7](https://ref.ly/Ps62:7) · [ISA 45:14](https://ref.ly/Isa45:14) · [ISA 45:24](https://ref.ly/Isa45:24) · [JER 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jer5:4) · [JER 10:24](https://ref.ly/Jer10:24) · [LAM 3:3](https://ref.ly/Lam3:3) · [EZK 46:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:17)

Affirmers

surely \= particle limiting the focus to one element and so enhancing its truth value

[GEN 26:9](https://ref.ly/Gen26:9) · [GEN 29:14](https://ref.ly/Gen29:14) · [GEN 44:28](https://ref.ly/Gen44:28) · [EXO 12:15](https://ref.ly/Exod12:15) · [EXO 31:13](https://ref.ly/Exod31:13) · [LEV 23:27](https://ref.ly/Lev23:27) · [LEV 23:39](https://ref.ly/Lev23:39) · [JDG 3:24](https://ref.ly/Judg3:24) · [JDG 20:39](https://ref.ly/Judg20:39) · [1SA 16:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:6) · [1KI 22:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:32) · [2KI 5:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:7) · [2KI 18:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:20) · [JOB 16:7](https://ref.ly/Job16:7) · [JOB 18:21](https://ref.ly/Job18:21) · [JOB 23:6](https://ref.ly/Job23:6) · [JOB 30:24](https://ref.ly/Job30:24) · [JOB 33:8](https://ref.ly/Job33:8) · [JOB 35:13](https://ref.ly/Job35:13) · [PSA 23:6](https://ref.ly/Ps23:6) · [PSA 58:12](https://ref.ly/Ps58:12) · [PSA 58:12](https://ref.ly/Ps58:12) · [PSA 75:9](https://ref.ly/Ps75:9) · [PSA 139:11](https://ref.ly/Ps139:11) · [PSA 140:14](https://ref.ly/Ps140:14) · [ISA 34:14](https://ref.ly/Isa34:14) · [ISA 34:15](https://ref.ly/Isa34:15) · [ISA 36:5](https://ref.ly/Isa36:5) · [ISA 63:8](https://ref.ly/Isa63:8) · [JER 2:35](https://ref.ly/Jer2:35) · [JER 10:19](https://ref.ly/Jer10:19) · [LAM 2:16](https://ref.ly/Lam2:16) · [HOS 12:9](https://ref.ly/Hos12:9) · [ZEP 3:7](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:7)

Contrastors

but \= particle marking an element as contrastive, and so limiting the scope or impact of what was stated before

[GEN 20:12](https://ref.ly/Gen20:12) · [DEU 18:20](https://ref.ly/Deut18:20) · [JOS 3:4](https://ref.ly/Josh3:4) · [1SA 12:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:20) · [1SA 29:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam29:9) · [2KI 12:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:14) · [2KI 13:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:6) · [2KI 22:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:7) · [2KI 23:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:9) · [2KI 23:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:26) · [2KI 23:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:35) · [PSA 49:16](https://ref.ly/Ps49:16) · [PSA 68:7](https://ref.ly/Ps68:7) · [PSA 68:22](https://ref.ly/Ps68:22) · [ISA 14:15](https://ref.ly/Isa14:15) · [ISA 43:24](https://ref.ly/Isa43:24) · [JER 5:5](https://ref.ly/Jer5:5) · [JER 12:1](https://ref.ly/Jer12:1) · [JER 26:24](https://ref.ly/Jer26:24) · [JER 28:7](https://ref.ly/Jer28:7) · [JER 30:11](https://ref.ly/Jer30:11) · [JER 34:4](https://ref.ly/Jer34:4) · [HOS 4:4](https://ref.ly/Hos4:4) · [JON 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:5)2 · [ZEC 1:6](https://ref.ly/Zech1:6)

Identifiers

nothing but, abundantly, utterly \= particle marking an element as prominent, as if limiting the focus to a restricted number of entities

[DEU 16:15](https://ref.ly/Deut16:15) · [DEU 28:29](https://ref.ly/Deut28:29) · [1SA 25:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:21) · [2KI 24:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:3) · [JOB 19:13](https://ref.ly/Job19:13) · [PSA 23:6](https://ref.ly/Ps23:6) · [PSA 37:8](https://ref.ly/Ps37:8) · [PSA 39:6](https://ref.ly/Ps39:6) · [PSA 39:7](https://ref.ly/Ps39:7) · [PSA 39:7](https://ref.ly/Ps39:7) · [PSA 39:12](https://ref.ly/Ps39:12) · [PSA 62:5](https://ref.ly/Ps62:5) · [PSA 62:10](https://ref.ly/Ps62:10) · [PSA 73:1](https://ref.ly/Ps73:1) · [PSA 73:13](https://ref.ly/Ps73:13) · [PSA 73:18](https://ref.ly/Ps73:18) · [PSA 85:10](https://ref.ly/Ps85:10) · [PRO 11:23](https://ref.ly/Prov11:23) · [PRO 11:24](https://ref.ly/Prov11:24) · [PRO 14:23](https://ref.ly/Prov14:23) · [PRO 17:11](https://ref.ly/Prov17:11) · [PRO 21:5](https://ref.ly/Prov21:5) · [PRO 21:5](https://ref.ly/Prov21:5) · [PRO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Prov22:16) · [ISA 16:7](https://ref.ly/Isa16:7) · [ISA 19:11](https://ref.ly/Isa19:11) · [JER 16:19](https://ref.ly/Jer16:19) · [JER 32:30](https://ref.ly/Jer32:30) · [JER 32:30](https://ref.ly/Jer32:30) · [HOS 12:12](https://ref.ly/Hos12:12) · [ZEP 1:18](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:18)

Stipulators

if only \= particle marking an event as a condition, and so limiting the scope or impact of what was stated before by making it dependent on another event

[GEN 23:13](https://ref.ly/Gen23:13) · [GEN 27:13](https://ref.ly/Gen27:13) · [GEN 34:23](https://ref.ly/Gen34:23) · [EXO 21:21](https://ref.ly/Exod21:21) · [NUM 14:9](https://ref.ly/Num14:9) · [NUM 26:55](https://ref.ly/Num26:55) · [NUM 31:23](https://ref.ly/Num31:23) · [DEU 12:22](https://ref.ly/Deut12:22) · [JOS 22:19](https://ref.ly/Josh22:19) · [JDG 10:15](https://ref.ly/Judg10:15) · [1SA 1:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:23) · [1SA 8:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:9) · [1SA 12:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:24) · [1SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:17) · [1KI 17:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:13) · [1CH 22:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:12) · [JOB 2:6](https://ref.ly/Job2:6) · [JER 3:13](https://ref.ly/Jer3:13) · [JER 26:15](https://ref.ly/Jer26:15)

Timers

as soon as \= particle marking a proposition as having very recently been completed, and so limiting the amount of time that could have passed between this event and the next

[GEN 27:30](https://ref.ly/Gen27:30) · [JDG 7:19](https://ref.ly/Judg7:19) · [1KI 9:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:24)

Ingest

to make (someone) eat, to give (someone) to eat \= action by which someone gives other people food for consumption, sometimes with the added connotation that these people are actually forced to consume it; with or without explicit object

[EXO 16:32](https://ref.ly/Exod16:32) · [NUM 11:4](https://ref.ly/Num11:4) · [NUM 11:18](https://ref.ly/Num11:18) · [DEU 8:3](https://ref.ly/Deut8:3) · [DEU 8:16](https://ref.ly/Deut8:16) · [1KI 22:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:27) · [2CH 18:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:26) · [2CH 28:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:15) · [PSA 80:6](https://ref.ly/Ps80:6) · [PSA 81:17](https://ref.ly/Ps81:17) · [PRO 25:21](https://ref.ly/Prov25:21) · [ISA 49:26](https://ref.ly/Isa49:26) · [JER 9:14](https://ref.ly/Jer9:14) · [JER 19:9](https://ref.ly/Jer19:9) · [JER 23:15](https://ref.ly/Jer23:15) · [EZK 3:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:2) · [EZK 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:3) · [EZK 16:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:19) · [HOS 11:4](https://ref.ly/Hos11:4)

Non\-Exist · Dead

to consume, to devour, to destroy \= action by which people or objects are removed from existence in a violent way ◄ as food that is eaten

Non\-Exist

to eat \> to enjoy, to eat \> to use up literally: to eat (goods); hence \= pattern of actions by which someone makes a living off material goods until they are gone; ≈ often associated with joy

[GEN 31:15](https://ref.ly/Gen31:15) · [GEN 31:15](https://ref.ly/Gen31:15) · [GEN 45:18](https://ref.ly/Gen45:18) · [JOB 20:21](https://ref.ly/Job20:21) · [JOB 21:25](https://ref.ly/Job21:25) · [PRO 13:2](https://ref.ly/Prov13:2) · [ECC 5:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:10) · [ECC 5:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:18) · [ECC 6:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:2) · [ECC 6:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:2) · [ISA 1:19](https://ref.ly/Isa1:19) · [ISA 61:6](https://ref.ly/Isa61:6)

Non\-Exist

to give to eat \> to give to enjoy literally: to give (goods) to eat; hence \= action by which someone enables someone else to make a living off material goods until they are gone; ≈ often associated with joy

[ISA 58:14](https://ref.ly/Isa58:14)

Non\-Space

to eat \> to take space literally: to eat space; hence: \= state in which something takes up space that would normally belong to another object

[EZK 42:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek42:5)

Possess

to eat \> to receive (a sacrifice) literally: to eat (an offering); hence: \= process by which a deity receives a burnt\-offering; ≈ used of idols only

[DEU 32:38](https://ref.ly/Deut32:38) · [EZK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:20)

Hear

**Collocation:** אכל דְּבָרִים

to eat words \> to listen carefully to (someone's) words literally: to eat words; hence: \= action by which someone listens carefully to someone else's words

[JER 15:16](https://ref.ly/Jer15:16)

Chastise

**Collocation:** אכל פְּרִי

to eat the fruits \> to bear the consequences literally: to eat the fruits (of something); hence: \= action by which someone accepts the consequences of an attitude or way of life ◄ as if they were the fruits of a tree; ≈ this expression can have both a positive or a negative sense

[PRO 1:31](https://ref.ly/Prov1:31) · [PRO 18:21](https://ref.ly/Prov18:21) · [ISA 3:10](https://ref.ly/Isa3:10) · [HOS 10:13](https://ref.ly/Hos10:13)

Revile

**Collocation:** אכל קְרַצִין

to denounce, to slander literally, to eat pieces (of someone)

[DAN 3:8](https://ref.ly/Dan3:8) · [DAN 6:25](https://ref.ly/Dan6:25)

1 ISA 37:30 MT\-K ואכול \| MT\-Q וְאִכְלוּ 2 JER 3:24 Most English versions interpret הַבֹּשֶׁת "the shame" as a reference to Baal (explicitly REB CEV GNT \| NJPS "the Shameful Thing") or idols (NIV "shameful gods"). Cf. also the commentaries of Rudolph, Holladay, Lundbom (but see Craigie/Kelly/Drinkard, following Seebass in TDOT II:55\). See also JER 11:13, HOS 9:10 for interchange between Boshet and Baal (relatively common also in proper names). 3 EZK 16:13 MT\-K אכלתי \| MT\-Q אָכָלְתְּ 4 EZK 44:3 MT\-K לאכול \| MT\-Q לֶאֱכָל 5 JER 51:34 MT\-K אַכָלָנוּ \| MT\-Q אַכָלַנִי 6 NEW NOTE en 7 NEW NOTE en 8 NEW NOTE en

## אָכֵן (id: 000376000000000)

אָכֵן
=====

אָכֵן (H0403)

intj

Affirmers

surely, certainly, truly \= marker used to affirm the truth of the following statement

[GEN 28:16](https://ref.ly/Gen28:16) · [EXO 2:14](https://ref.ly/Exod2:14) · [1SA 15:32](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:32) · [1KI 11:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:2) · [ISA 40:7](https://ref.ly/Isa40:7) · [JER 3:23](https://ref.ly/Jer3:23) · [JER 3:23](https://ref.ly/Jer3:23)

Affirmers · Contrastors

however, yet, but, but truly, the truth is, nevertheless \= marker used to affirm the truth of the following statement, which is in contrast with what was assumed to be the truth earlier on

Deities

divine being, heavenly being, god \= generic term for a supernatural being, worshiped by individuals or entire nations

Names of Deities

**Collocation:** אֵל עֹולָם

El Olam, Everlasting God \= a biblical title for the highest God, creator of heaven and earth

[GEN 21:33](https://ref.ly/Gen21:33)

Names of Deities

**Collocation:** אֵל עֶלְיֹון

El Elyon, God Most High \= a biblical title for the highest God, creator of heaven and earth

[GEN 14:18](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18) · [GEN 14:19](https://ref.ly/Gen14:19) · [GEN 14:20](https://ref.ly/Gen14:20) · [GEN 14:22](https://ref.ly/Gen14:22) · [PSA 78:35](https://ref.ly/Ps78:35)

Names of Deities

**Collocation:** אֵל רָאִי

El\-roi, the God who sees me \= a biblical title for the highest God, creator of heaven and earth

[GEN 16:13](https://ref.ly/Gen16:13)

Names of Deities

**Collocation:** אֵל שַׁדַּי

El Shaddai, God Almighty \= a biblical title for the highest God, creator of heaven and earth; exact meaning unsure, traditionally rendered as God Almighty

[GEN 17:1](https://ref.ly/Gen17:1) · [GEN 28:3](https://ref.ly/Gen28:3) · [GEN 35:11](https://ref.ly/Gen35:11) · [GEN 43:14](https://ref.ly/Gen43:14) · [GEN 48:3](https://ref.ly/Gen48:3) · [EXO 6:3](https://ref.ly/Exod6:3) · [EZK 10:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek10:5)

Names of Constructions

**Collocation:** אֵל אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל

El\-elohe\-yisrael, God, the God of Israel \= name of altar, ► erected by Jacob

[GEN 33:20](https://ref.ly/Gen33:20)

Names of Constructions

**Collocation:** אֵל בֵּית־אֵל

El\-bethel, God of Bethel \= name of altar, ► erected by Jacob

[GEN 35:7](https://ref.ly/Gen35:7)

Large

mighty, large, great (in size) \= state of being impressive in size, evoking an association with God

[PSA 36:7](https://ref.ly/Ps36:7)1 · [PSA 80:11](https://ref.ly/Ps80:11)2 · [ISA 14:13](https://ref.ly/Isa14:13)3

Able

**Collocation:** אֵל יָד

power, possibility literally: power of (one's) hand; hence: \= the capacity to perform a certain event

[GEN 31:29](https://ref.ly/Gen31:29) · [DEU 28:32](https://ref.ly/Deut28:32) · [NEH 5:5](https://ref.ly/Neh5:5) · [PRO 3:27](https://ref.ly/Prov3:27) · [MIC 2:1](https://ref.ly/Mic2:1)

dem.pron

Identifiers

these, those \= marker identifying a set of multiple objects with a set that already exists in the mind of the hearers

[GEN 19:8](https://ref.ly/Gen19:8) · [GEN 19:25](https://ref.ly/Gen19:25) · [GEN 26:3](https://ref.ly/Gen26:3) · [GEN 26:4](https://ref.ly/Gen26:4) · [LEV 18:27](https://ref.ly/Lev18:27) · [DEU 4:42](https://ref.ly/Deut4:42) · [DEU 7:22](https://ref.ly/Deut7:22) · [DEU 19:11](https://ref.ly/Deut19:11) · [1CH 20:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr20:8) · [EZR 5:15](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:15)

Rain

## אלה (id: 000390000000000)

אלה
===

אלה (H0421 · H0422)

verb · אָלָה · תַּאֲלָה

Curse

to utter a curse, to bind with a curse \= action by which someone invokes the name of a deity and ask him to punish a wrong\-doer; ◄ contexts in which this may happen include: (1\) covenants and agreements, where the deity is asked to punish those who do not obey the terms of the agreement, (2\) cases where a supposedly wrongful act has been committed, but where there is not enough certainty about the identity of the person who is responsible, and (3\) in order to prevent people from committing a wrongful action, knowing that the deity's punishment will be upon them if they do so; ► the effectiveness of the curse partially depends on the authority of the one pronouncing it

[JDG 17:2](https://ref.ly/Judg17:2) · [HOS 10:4](https://ref.ly/Hos10:4)

Curse

to cause someone to bind himself with a curse \= to cause someone else to invoke the name of a deity and ask him to punish a wrong\-doer; ◄ contexts in which this may happen include: (1\) covenants and agreements, where the deity is asked to punish those who do not obey the terms of the agreement, (2\) cases where a supposedly wrongful act has been committed, but where there is not enough certainty about the identity of the person who is responsible, and (3\) in order to prevent people from committing a wrongful action, knowing that the deity's punishment will be upon them if they do so; ► the effectiveness of the curse partially depends on the authority of the one pronouncing it

[1SA 14:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:24) · [1KI 8:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:31) · [2CH 6:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:22)

Curse

to curse \= to consign someone or something to death, damage, or destruction, usually by invoking the name of a deity if the agent is human; ◄ this is done as punishment, out of contempt, anger, or hostility; ► the effectiveness of a curse partially depends on the authority of the one pronouncing it

[HOS 4:2](https://ref.ly/Hos4:2)

verb

Shout

to lament \= to loudly express one's grief

[JOL 1:8](https://ref.ly/Joel1:8)

verb

Test

to try \= to make an effort to perform a certain event

[1SA 17:39](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:39)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 17:2; Hosea 10:4; 1 Samuel 14:24; 1 Kings 8:31; 2 Chronicles 6:22; Hosea 4:2; Joel 1:8; 1 Samuel 17:39

## אֵלָה (id: 000392000000000)

אֵלָה
=====

אֵלָה (H0424 · H0425) — אֵילָה

noun f

Trees

terebinth \= terebinth; tree with a broad crown, heavy branches, and thick trunk; may reach a height of 7\.5 to 9 m.; sheds its leaves; has small an inconspicuous flowers; ◄ pictacia terebinthus; ► its stem contains aromatic turpentine, a resinous juice, which flows out when the bark is cut; it provides pleasant shade; ≈ imposing tree, preferred place to sit under and enjoy the shade, associated with cultic worship

[GEN 35:4](https://ref.ly/Gen35:4) · [JDG 6:11](https://ref.ly/Judg6:11) · [JDG 6:19](https://ref.ly/Judg6:19) · [2SA 18:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:9) · [2SA 18:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:9) · [2SA 18:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:10) · [2SA 18:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:14) · [1KI 13:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:14) · [1CH 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:12) · [ISA 1:30](https://ref.ly/Isa1:30) · [ISA 6:13](https://ref.ly/Isa6:13) · [EZK 6:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:13) · [HOS 4:13](https://ref.ly/Hos4:13)

name

Names of People

Elah \= man; ► chief of Edom

[GEN 36:41](https://ref.ly/Gen36:41) · [1CH 1:52](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:52)

Names of Locations

Elah \= valley ► where David killed Goliath

Names of People

Elah \= man; ◄ son of Baasha; ► king of Israel (886/85–885/84\)

[1KI 16:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:6) · [1KI 16:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:8) · [1KI 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:13) · [1KI 16:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:14)

Names of People

Elah \= man; ► father of Hoshea, king of Israel

[2KI 15:30](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:30) · [2KI 17:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:1) · [2KI 18:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:1) · [2KI 18:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:9)

Names of People

Elah \= man; ◄ son of Caleb; tribe of Judah

[1CH 4:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:15) · [1CH 4:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:15)

Names of People

Elah \= man; ◄ son of Uzzi; tribe of Benjamin; ► after exile

## אַלָּה (id: 000394000000000)

אַלָּה
======

אַלָּה (H0427)

noun f

Trees

terebinth \= tree, more commonly known as אֵלָה, with a broad crown, heavy branches, and thick trunk; may reach a height of 7\.5 to 9 m.; sheds its leaves; has small an inconspicuous flowers; ◄ pictacia terebinthus; ► its stem contains aromatic turpentine, a resinous juice, which flows out when the bark is cut; it provides pleasant shade; ≈ imposing tree, preferred place to sit under and enjoy the shade, associated with cultic worship

[JOS 24:26](https://ref.ly/Josh24:26)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Joshua 24:26


## אָלָה (id: 000395000000000)

אָלָה
=====

אָלָה (H0423)

noun f · אלה

Vow

oath, curse \= action by which the name of a deity is invoked and asked to punish a wrong\-doer; ◄ contexts in which this may happen include: (1\) covenants and agreements, where the deity is asked to punish those who do not obey the terms of the agreement, (2\) cases where a supposedly wrongful act has been committed, but where there is not enough certainty about the identity of the person who is responsible, and (3\) in order to prevent people from committing a wrongful action, knowing that the deity's punishment will be upon them if they do so

[GEN 24:41](https://ref.ly/Gen24:41) · [GEN 24:41](https://ref.ly/Gen24:41) · [GEN 26:28](https://ref.ly/Gen26:28) · [LEV 5:1](https://ref.ly/Lev5:1) · [NUM 5:21](https://ref.ly/Num5:21) · [NUM 5:23](https://ref.ly/Num5:23) · [DEU 29:11](https://ref.ly/Deut29:11) · [DEU 29:13](https://ref.ly/Deut29:13) · [DEU 29:18](https://ref.ly/Deut29:18) · [DEU 29:19](https://ref.ly/Deut29:19) · [DEU 29:20](https://ref.ly/Deut29:20) · [DEU 30:7](https://ref.ly/Deut30:7) · [1KI 8:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:31) · [1KI 8:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:31) · [2CH 6:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:22) · [2CH 6:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:22) · [2CH 34:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:24) · [NEH 10:30](https://ref.ly/Neh10:30) · [PRO 29:24](https://ref.ly/Prov29:24) · [ISA 24:6](https://ref.ly/Isa24:6) · [JER 23:10](https://ref.ly/Jer23:10) · [EZK 16:59](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:59) · [EZK 17:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:13) · [EZK 17:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:16) · [EZK 17:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:18) · [EZK 17:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:19) · [DAN 9:11](https://ref.ly/Dan9:11) · [ZEC 5:3](https://ref.ly/Zech5:3)

Curse

curse \= action by which someone or something is consigned to death, damage, or destruction, usually by invoking the name of a deity if the agent is human; ◄ this is done as punishment, out of contempt, anger, or hostility; ► the effectiveness of a curse partially depends on the authority of the one pronouncing it

[JOB 31:30](https://ref.ly/Job31:30) · [PSA 10:7](https://ref.ly/Ps10:7) · [PSA 59:13](https://ref.ly/Ps59:13)

Curse

curse \= object of a curse uttered by other people because of the wrongful acts that one has committed

[NUM 5:21](https://ref.ly/Num5:21) · [NUM 5:27](https://ref.ly/Num5:27) · [JER 29:18](https://ref.ly/Jer29:18) · [JER 42:18](https://ref.ly/Jer42:18) · [JER 44:12](https://ref.ly/Jer44:12)

Prophets

**Collocation:** אִישׁ (הָ)אֱלֹהִים

man of God \= person who is closely associated with God, who speaks on behalf of God, and through whom inquiries can be made of God

Professions

judges \= people with the duty of judging people's cases; ≈ perhaps seen as representatives of God on earth

[EXO 21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:6)21 · [EXO 22:7](https://ref.ly/Exod22:7)21 · [EXO 22:8](https://ref.ly/Exod22:8)21 · [EXO 22:8](https://ref.ly/Exod22:8)21

Large

mighty, large, great (in size) \= state in which an object is impressive in size, evoking an association with God

[PSA 68:16](https://ref.ly/Ps68:16)23 · [JON 3:3](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:3)25

Quantity

mighty (in number) \= state in which objects are impressive in number, evoking an association with God

[PSA 68:18](https://ref.ly/Ps68:18)24

Great

mighty (in status) \= state in which a human being is highly esteemd, evoking an association with God

[GEN 23:6](https://ref.ly/Gen23:6)

Intense

mighty, exceedingly, great (in intensity) \= process by which an event takes place with great intensity, evoking an association with God

[GEN 1:2](https://ref.ly/Gen1:2)18 · [GEN 23:6](https://ref.ly/Gen23:6) · [GEN 30:8](https://ref.ly/Gen30:8) · [GEN 35:5](https://ref.ly/Gen35:5)19 · [1SA 14:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:15)22

Just

godly, holy \= state in which humans are characterized as pure and undefiled in character, evoking an association with God

[MAL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Mal2:15)26

## אֵלֹון (id: 000401000000000)

אֵלֹון
======

אֵלֹון (H0436)

noun m · אַלֹּון · אֵלֹנִי

Trees

oak \= a large\-size tree found either alone or in clumps or forests; ◄ one of the varieties of \|iQuercus\|i\*; ► functioned as landmarks, grave marks or gathering places; since its groves were considered sacred by Canaanites, ritual practices under them was always a temptation to Israelites, denounced in the texts as idolatry; ≈ their characteristics made them suitable symbols of a variety of attributes or attitudes

[JOS 19:33](https://ref.ly/Josh19:33) · [JDG 4:11](https://ref.ly/Judg4:11) · [JDG 9:6](https://ref.ly/Judg9:6)

name

Names of People

Elon \= man; ◄ son of Zebulun

[GEN 46:14](https://ref.ly/Gen46:14) · [NUM 26:26](https://ref.ly/Num26:26)

Names of Trees

**Collocation:** אֵלֹון מֹורֶה

Oak of More \= oak tree; ◄ located near Shechem; ► one of the places where Abraham pitched his tents

[GEN 12:6](https://ref.ly/Gen12:6) · [DEU 11:30](https://ref.ly/Deut11:30)

Names of Trees

**Collocation:** אֵלֹנֵי מַמְרֵא

Oaks of Mamre \= group of oak trees; ◄ located near Hebron; ► one of the places where Abraham pitched his tents

[GEN 13:18](https://ref.ly/Gen13:18) · [GEN 14:13](https://ref.ly/Gen14:13) · [GEN 18:1](https://ref.ly/Gen18:1)

Names of Trees

**Collocation:** אֵלֹון מְעֹונְנִים

Diviner's Oak \= oak tree; ◄ located near Shechem

[JDG 9:37](https://ref.ly/Judg9:37)

Names of Trees

**Collocation:** אֵלֹון תָּבֹור

Oak of Tabor \= oak tree; ◄ located near Bethel

[1SA 10:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:3)

Reinier de Blois

verb · אִלֵּם · אֵלֶם · אַלְמָן · אַלְמָנָה · אַלְמָנוּת · אַלְמֹנִי

Unable

to be dumb, to be unable to speak \= to be unable to speak ◄ because of a physical limitation or because of emotions

[PSA 31:19](https://ref.ly/Ps31:19) · [PSA 39:3](https://ref.ly/Ps39:3) · [PSA 39:10](https://ref.ly/Ps39:10) · [ISA 53:7](https://ref.ly/Isa53:7) · [EZK 3:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:26) · [EZK 24:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:27) · [EZK 33:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:22) · [DAN 10:15](https://ref.ly/Dan10:15)

verb · אֲלֻמָּה

Bind

**Collocation:** אלם אֲלֻמָּה

to make sheaves \= action by which someone bundles harvested ears of grain

[GEN 37:7](https://ref.ly/Gen37:7)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 31:19; Psalms 39:3; Psalms 39:10; Isaiah 53:7; Ezekiel 3:26; Ezekiel 24:27; Ezekiel 33:22; Daniel 10:15; Genesis 37:7

## אלף (id: 000466000000000)

אלף
===

אלף (H0502 · H0503)

verb · אַלּוּף · אֶלֶף

Know

to learn \= to acquire knowledge of something

[PRO 22:25](https://ref.ly/Prov22:25)

Know

to give knowledge, to teach \= to impart knowledge to someone else

[JOB 15:5](https://ref.ly/Job15:5) · [JOB 33:33](https://ref.ly/Job33:33) · [JOB 35:11](https://ref.ly/Job35:11)

verb · אֶלֶף

Quantity

to be thousands in number, to become thousands in number \= to be or become thousands in number

[PSA 144:13](https://ref.ly/Ps144:13)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 22:25; Job 15:5; Job 33:33; Job 35:11; Psalms 144:13


## אֲלַף (id: 000467000000000)

אֲלַף
=====

אֲלַף (A0506)

noun c

Quantity · Frequency

thousand \= pertaining to being a thousand in number or to happening a thousand times

[DAN 5:1](https://ref.ly/Dan5:1) · [DAN 5:1](https://ref.ly/Dan5:1) · [DAN 7:10](https://ref.ly/Dan7:10) · [DAN 7:10](https://ref.ly/Dan7:10)

Reinier de Blois

אֶלֶף

noun m · אלף

Domestic Animals

cows, cattle \= generic term for domesticated oxen, both male and female; ► kept for milk and used for plowing

אֵם
===

אֵם (H0517)

noun f

Relatives

mother \= direct female progenitor; ≈ associated with affection and loyalty; often extended figuratively to refer to other objects that are regarded as feminine

Relatives

mother \= indirect female progenitor

[GEN 3:20](https://ref.ly/Gen3:20)

Groups

**Collocation:** בֵּית אֵם

"mother's house" \= mother's household \= members of an extended family centered around one of the wives, together with other people associated with them, such as servants or slaves

[GEN 24:28](https://ref.ly/Gen24:28)

Groups

mother \> place of origin and sustenance \= a nation or a city representing a nation, ◄ here compared to a mother, and its citizens to her children who are responsible for her well\-being

[ISA 50:1](https://ref.ly/Isa50:1) · [ISA 50:1](https://ref.ly/Isa50:1) · [JER 50:12](https://ref.ly/Jer50:12) · [EZK 19:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:2) · [EZK 19:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:10) · [HOS 2:4](https://ref.ly/Hos2:4) · [HOS 2:7](https://ref.ly/Hos2:7) · [HOS 4:5](https://ref.ly/Hos4:5)

Faithful

mother \> faithful (town) \= state in which a town is committed to fulfilling its obligations to the nation of which it is a part; ◄ here compared to a mother, who is considered a loyal and responsible part of a family

[2SA 20:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:19)

Titles

mother \> friend \= friendly and endearing way to address a woman

[JOB 17:14](https://ref.ly/Job17:14)

Roads

**Collocation:** אֵם הַדֶּרֶךְ

"the mother of the way" \= the parting of a way \= the place where a road splits into two or more other roads; ◄ here compared to a mother with multiple children

[EZK 21:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:26)

1 GEN 37:10 A special meaning "step\-mother" here (HALOT, Holladay) is not required. 2 1KI 15:10 A special meaning "grandmother" has been suggested here (HALOT, Holladay \| NAB, NIV, REB), due to apparent contradictions in the narrative. But see Cogan, 1 Kings, ad. loc.

Enio R. Mueller
