## אַמָּה (id: 000481000000000)

אַמָּה
======

אַמָּה (H0520 · A0521 · H0522)

noun f

Dimension

cubit \= linear measure, corresponding to the length of the forearm, from the tip of the middle finger to the point of the elbow; the exact measure is uncertain; apparently there were two standards: the normal, estimated as 44\.5 cm; and the royal, estimated as 51\.81 cm

noun f · אמן

Non\-Move

steadiness \= state in which an object does not move

[EXO 17:12](https://ref.ly/Exod17:12)

Endure

stability \= state in which the circumstances of life do not change in any significant way; ≈ associated with security and well\-being

[PSA 37:3](https://ref.ly/Ps37:3) · [ISA 33:6](https://ref.ly/Isa33:6)

Faithful

faithfulness, reliability, trustworthiness \= state in which humans and deities are considered worthy of trust, because they are truthful and committed, with an unwavering disposition, which is reflected in their actions

[DEU 32:4](https://ref.ly/Deut32:4) · [1SA 26:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:23) · [2KI 12:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:16) · [2KI 22:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:7) · [2CH 19:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:9) · [2CH 31:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:12) · [2CH 31:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:15) · [2CH 31:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:18) · [2CH 34:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:12) · [PSA 33:4](https://ref.ly/Ps33:4) · [PSA 36:6](https://ref.ly/Ps36:6) · [PSA 40:11](https://ref.ly/Ps40:11) · [PSA 88:12](https://ref.ly/Ps88:12) · [PSA 89:2](https://ref.ly/Ps89:2) · [PSA 89:3](https://ref.ly/Ps89:3) · [PSA 89:6](https://ref.ly/Ps89:6) · [PSA 89:9](https://ref.ly/Ps89:9) · [PSA 89:25](https://ref.ly/Ps89:25) · [PSA 89:34](https://ref.ly/Ps89:34) · [PSA 89:50](https://ref.ly/Ps89:50) · [PSA 92:3](https://ref.ly/Ps92:3) · [PSA 96:13](https://ref.ly/Ps96:13) · [PSA 98:3](https://ref.ly/Ps98:3) · [PSA 100:5](https://ref.ly/Ps100:5) · [PSA 119:30](https://ref.ly/Ps119:30) · [PSA 119:75](https://ref.ly/Ps119:75) · [PSA 119:86](https://ref.ly/Ps119:86) · [PSA 119:90](https://ref.ly/Ps119:90) · [PSA 119:138](https://ref.ly/Ps119:138) · [PSA 143:1](https://ref.ly/Ps143:1) · [PRO 12:17](https://ref.ly/Prov12:17) · [PRO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Prov12:22) · [PRO 28:20](https://ref.ly/Prov28:20) · [ISA 11:5](https://ref.ly/Isa11:5) · [ISA 25:1](https://ref.ly/Isa25:1) · [ISA 59:4](https://ref.ly/Isa59:4) · [JER 5:1](https://ref.ly/Jer5:1) · [JER 5:3](https://ref.ly/Jer5:3) · [JER 7:28](https://ref.ly/Jer7:28) · [JER 9:2](https://ref.ly/Jer9:2) · [LAM 3:23](https://ref.ly/Lam3:23) · [HOS 2:22](https://ref.ly/Hos2:22) · [HAB 2:4](https://ref.ly/Hab2:4)

Role

position, commission, responsibility, office of trust \= state in which humans have been entrusted with a certain responsibility, which requires commitment and faithfulness

[1CH 9:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:22) · [1CH 9:26](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:26) · [1CH 9:31](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:31)

## אמל (id: 000491000000000)

אמל
===

אמל (H0535) — אֲמֻלָה

verb · אֻמְלַל · אֲמֵלָל

Weak

to languish, to wither \= state of being unable to function as can be expected; ◄ as a result of destruction, a drought, or God's direct intervention; ► results include weakness, sadness, shame, death, and disintegration, depending on the situation

[1SA 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:5) · [ISA 16:8](https://ref.ly/Isa16:8) · [ISA 19:8](https://ref.ly/Isa19:8) · [ISA 24:4](https://ref.ly/Isa24:4) · [ISA 24:4](https://ref.ly/Isa24:4) · [ISA 24:7](https://ref.ly/Isa24:7) · [ISA 33:9](https://ref.ly/Isa33:9) · [JER 14:2](https://ref.ly/Jer14:2) · [JER 15:9](https://ref.ly/Jer15:9) · [LAM 2:8](https://ref.ly/Lam2:8) · [EZK 16:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:30) · [HOS 4:3](https://ref.ly/Hos4:3) · [JOL 1:10](https://ref.ly/Joel1:10) · [JOL 1:12](https://ref.ly/Joel1:12) · [NAM 1:4](https://ref.ly/Nah1:4) · [NAM 1:4](https://ref.ly/Nah1:4)

## אמן (id: 000495000000000)

אמן
===

אמן (H0539 · H0540)

verb · אָמֵן · אֹמֶן · אֲמָנָה · אֹמְנָה · אָמְנָם · אֻמְנָם · אֵמוּן · אֱמוּנָה · אֱמֶת · אָמֹון · אֲמָנָה · אַמְנֹון · אֲמִתַּי

Non\-Move

firm \= state in which an inanimate object is firmly positioned in its place, so that it will not move

[ISA 22:23](https://ref.ly/Isa22:23) · [ISA 22:25](https://ref.ly/Isa22:25)

Non\-Move

to stand still \= action in which an animate object stays in one place without moving

[JOB 39:24](https://ref.ly/Job39:24)

Confident

to be confident \= state in which humans are confident that they can face the situations that they are confronted with, and are not given to fear

[2CH 20:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:20) · [ISA 7:9](https://ref.ly/Isa7:9)

Confident

to be convinced, to believe \= state in which humans are confident with regard to another person or event, which means that this person is believed to be speaking the truth, or that this event is true

[GEN 45:26](https://ref.ly/Gen45:26) · [EXO 4:1](https://ref.ly/Exod4:1) · [EXO 4:5](https://ref.ly/Exod4:5) · [EXO 4:8](https://ref.ly/Exod4:8) · [EXO 4:8](https://ref.ly/Exod4:8) · [EXO 4:9](https://ref.ly/Exod4:9) · [EXO 4:31](https://ref.ly/Exod4:31) · [DEU 9:23](https://ref.ly/Deut9:23) · [1KI 10:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:7) · [2CH 9:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:6) · [2CH 32:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:15) · [JOB 9:16](https://ref.ly/Job9:16) · [JOB 15:22](https://ref.ly/Job15:22) · [JOB 29:24](https://ref.ly/Job29:24) · [PSA 27:13](https://ref.ly/Ps27:13) · [PSA 106:24](https://ref.ly/Ps106:24) · [PRO 14:15](https://ref.ly/Prov14:15) · [ISA 43:10](https://ref.ly/Isa43:10) · [ISA 53:1](https://ref.ly/Isa53:1) · [JER 40:14](https://ref.ly/Jer40:14) · [LAM 4:12](https://ref.ly/Lam4:12) · [HAB 1:5](https://ref.ly/Hab1:5)

Confident

to have faith, to keep faith, to trust, to believe \= state in which humans are confident with regard to another person or deity, which means that this person or deity does only speak the truth but is also faithful, and willing and able to help

[GEN 15:6](https://ref.ly/Gen15:6) · [EXO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Exod14:31) · [EXO 19:9](https://ref.ly/Exod19:9) · [NUM 14:11](https://ref.ly/Num14:11) · [NUM 20:12](https://ref.ly/Num20:12) · [DEU 1:32](https://ref.ly/Deut1:32) · [DEU 28:66](https://ref.ly/Deut28:66) · [JDG 11:20](https://ref.ly/Judg11:20) · [1SA 27:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:12) · [2KI 17:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:14) · [2CH 20:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:20) · [2CH 20:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:20) · [JOB 4:18](https://ref.ly/Job4:18) · [JOB 15:15](https://ref.ly/Job15:15) · [JOB 15:31](https://ref.ly/Job15:31) · [JOB 24:22](https://ref.ly/Job24:22) · [JOB 39:12](https://ref.ly/Job39:12) · [PSA 78:22](https://ref.ly/Ps78:22) · [PSA 78:32](https://ref.ly/Ps78:32) · [PSA 106:12](https://ref.ly/Ps106:12) · [PSA 116:10](https://ref.ly/Ps116:10) · [PSA 119:66](https://ref.ly/Ps119:66) · [PRO 26:25](https://ref.ly/Prov26:25) · [ISA 7:9](https://ref.ly/Isa7:9) · [ISA 28:16](https://ref.ly/Isa28:16) · [JER 12:6](https://ref.ly/Jer12:6) · [DAN 6:24](https://ref.ly/Dan6:24) · [JON 3:5](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:5) · [MIC 7:5](https://ref.ly/Mic7:5)

Endure

to last, to endure, lasting, enduring, chronic \= state in which an event or condition endures for an extended period of time

[DEU 28:59](https://ref.ly/Deut28:59) · [DEU 28:59](https://ref.ly/Deut28:59) · [1SA 2:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:35) · [1SA 25:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:28) · [2SA 7:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:16) · [1KI 11:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:38) · [1CH 17:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:24) · [PSA 19:8](https://ref.ly/Ps19:8) · [PSA 89:29](https://ref.ly/Ps89:29) · [PSA 93:5](https://ref.ly/Ps93:5) · [PSA 111:7](https://ref.ly/Ps111:7) · [PRO 27:6](https://ref.ly/Prov27:6) · [ISA 33:16](https://ref.ly/Isa33:16) · [ISA 55:3](https://ref.ly/Isa55:3) · [JER 15:18](https://ref.ly/Jer15:18) · [HOS 5:9](https://ref.ly/Hos5:9)

Faithful

to be faithful, to be reliable, to be trustworthy, to be true \= state in which humans and deities are considered worthy of trust, because they are truthful and committed, with an unwavering disposition, which is reflected in their words and actions

[NUM 12:7](https://ref.ly/Num12:7) · [DEU 7:9](https://ref.ly/Deut7:9) · [1SA 2:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:35) · [1SA 3:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:20) · [1SA 22:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:14) · [NEH 9:8](https://ref.ly/Neh9:8) · [NEH 13:13](https://ref.ly/Neh13:13) · [JOB 12:20](https://ref.ly/Job12:20) · [PSA 78:8](https://ref.ly/Ps78:8) · [PSA 78:37](https://ref.ly/Ps78:37) · [PSA 89:38](https://ref.ly/Ps89:38) · [PSA 101:6](https://ref.ly/Ps101:6) · [PRO 11:13](https://ref.ly/Prov11:13) · [PRO 25:13](https://ref.ly/Prov25:13) · [ISA 1:21](https://ref.ly/Isa1:21) · [ISA 1:26](https://ref.ly/Isa1:26) · [ISA 8:2](https://ref.ly/Isa8:2) · [ISA 49:7](https://ref.ly/Isa49:7) · [JER 42:5](https://ref.ly/Jer42:5) · [DAN 6:5](https://ref.ly/Dan6:5) · [HOS 12:1](https://ref.ly/Hos12:1)

True

to be true, to be reliable, to be fulfilled, to be verified, to be confirmed \= process by which someone's words turn out to be true

[GEN 42:20](https://ref.ly/Gen42:20) · [1KI 8:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:26) · [1CH 17:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:23) · [2CH 1:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:9) · [2CH 6:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:17) · [DAN 2:45](https://ref.ly/Dan2:45)

verb

Care

to be brought up, to be raised \= process of being raised and educated from childhood to grown age

[LAM 4:5](https://ref.ly/Lam4:5)

Care

nurse, foster\-parent, guardian \= man or woman responsible for raising and educating one or more children on behalf of the parents

[NUM 11:12](https://ref.ly/Num11:12) · [RUT 4:16](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:16) · [2SA 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:4) · [2KI 10:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:1) · [2KI 10:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:5) · [EST 2:7](https://ref.ly/Esth2:7) · [ISA 49:23](https://ref.ly/Isa49:23)

Hold

to be carried (on the hip) \= process by which a child is carried and cared for by a nurse

[ISA 60:4](https://ref.ly/Isa60:4)

Strong

[GEN 25:23](https://ref.ly/Gen25:23) · [2SA 22:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:18) · [2CH 13:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:18) · [PSA 18:18](https://ref.ly/Ps18:18) · [PSA 142:7](https://ref.ly/Ps142:7)

Strong

to summon one's strength \= process by which humans summon their physical strength to accomplish a particular goal

[1KI 12:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:18) · [2CH 10:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:18) · [PRO 24:5](https://ref.ly/Prov24:5) · [AMO 2:14](https://ref.ly/Amos2:14) · [NAM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Nah2:2)

Strong

to strengthen, to support, to repair, to let grow strong \= causative action by which humans or deities cause inanimate objects or (other) humans to have physical, political, and/or military strength

[2CH 11:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:17) · [2CH 24:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:13) · [PSA 80:16](https://ref.ly/Ps80:16) · [PSA 80:18](https://ref.ly/Ps80:18) · [PSA 89:22](https://ref.ly/Ps89:22) · [PRO 8:28](https://ref.ly/Prov8:28) · [PRO 31:17](https://ref.ly/Prov31:17) · [ISA 35:3](https://ref.ly/Isa35:3) · [ISA 41:10](https://ref.ly/Isa41:10) · [ISA 44:14](https://ref.ly/Isa44:14)

Confident

to be strong, to be courageous \= state in which humans have the inner strength to face difficult circumstances

[DEU 31:6](https://ref.ly/Deut31:6) · [DEU 31:7](https://ref.ly/Deut31:7) · [DEU 31:23](https://ref.ly/Deut31:23) · [JOS 1:6](https://ref.ly/Josh1:6) · [JOS 1:7](https://ref.ly/Josh1:7) · [JOS 1:9](https://ref.ly/Josh1:9) · [JOS 1:18](https://ref.ly/Josh1:18) · [JOS 10:25](https://ref.ly/Josh10:25) · [1CH 22:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:13) · [1CH 28:20](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:20) · [2CH 32:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:7) · [PSA 27:14](https://ref.ly/Ps27:14) · [PSA 31:25](https://ref.ly/Ps31:25)

Confident

to make a firm decision, to be determined, to defy \= process by which humans summon their inner strength to accomplish a particular goal; ≈ this can have both a positive (determination) and a negative (defiance) sense

[RUT 1:18](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:18) · [2CH 13:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:7)

Confident

to make strong, to encourage, to harden \= state in which humans help other humans to find the inner strength to face difficult circumstances; ≈ this can have both a positive (courage) and a negative (stubbornness) sense

[DEU 2:30](https://ref.ly/Deut2:30) · [DEU 3:28](https://ref.ly/Deut3:28) · [DEU 15:7](https://ref.ly/Deut15:7) · [2CH 36:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:13) · [JOB 4:4](https://ref.ly/Job4:4) · [JOB 16:5](https://ref.ly/Job16:5)

Speak

to mention \= action by which humans refer to a particular object when speaking

[GEN 22:2](https://ref.ly/Gen22:2) · [GEN 43:27](https://ref.ly/Gen43:27) · [GEN 43:29](https://ref.ly/Gen43:29) · [NUM 14:40](https://ref.ly/Num14:40) · [2SA 6:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:22) · [NEH 6:19](https://ref.ly/Neh6:19) · [EST 2:13](https://ref.ly/Esth2:13) · [EST 2:15](https://ref.ly/Esth2:15) · [PSA 40:11](https://ref.ly/Ps40:11) · [PSA 41:6](https://ref.ly/Ps41:6) · [PSA 45:2](https://ref.ly/Ps45:2) · [PSA 145:6](https://ref.ly/Ps145:6) · [PSA 145:11](https://ref.ly/Ps145:11) · [PRO 1:21](https://ref.ly/Prov1:21) · [JER 23:38](https://ref.ly/Jer23:38) · [JER 31:23](https://ref.ly/Jer31:23) · [EZK 13:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:7)

Speak

to assign \= action by which humans give verbal instructions regarding something

[1KI 11:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:18) · [2CH 29:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:24)

Speak

to declare, to affirm \= action by which humans and deities make a very solemn statement concerning something

[DEU 26:17](https://ref.ly/Deut26:17) · [DEU 26:18](https://ref.ly/Deut26:18)

Great

to boast \= action by which humans utter words about themselves, usually with the purpose of drawing all attention to themselves

[PSA 94:4](https://ref.ly/Ps94:4) · [ISA 61:6](https://ref.ly/Isa61:6)7

אנה

Happen

**Collocation:** אנה לְיָד

to allow to happen literally: to cause to happen to (someone's) hand; hence: causative process by which deities cause or allow something to happen to somebody

Conflict

to pick a quarrel \= action by which humans look for an occasion to start a conflict with other humans

אנס
===

verb

Engage

to force, to compel, to restrain \= to enforce a certain type of behavior

to be too difficult, to baffle \= state in which a mystery is too hard for someone to understand

## אנף (id: 000547000000000)

אנף
===

אנף (H0599)

verb · אַף · אַפַּיִם

Angry

to be angry, to become angry \= process by which deities or humans experience an emotion of anger; ◄ caused by someone else's behavior ► almost invariably resulting in punishment for the party that is responsible, and almost exclusively used with God as the subject

[DEU 1:37](https://ref.ly/Deut1:37) · [DEU 4:21](https://ref.ly/Deut4:21) · [DEU 9:8](https://ref.ly/Deut9:8) · [DEU 9:20](https://ref.ly/Deut9:20) · [1KI 8:46](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:46) · [1KI 11:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:9) · [2KI 17:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:18) · [2CH 6:36](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:36) · [EZR 9:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:14) · [PSA 2:12](https://ref.ly/Ps2:12) · [PSA 60:3](https://ref.ly/Ps60:3) · [PSA 79:5](https://ref.ly/Ps79:5) · [PSA 85:6](https://ref.ly/Ps85:6) · [ISA 12:1](https://ref.ly/Isa12:1)

Christopher J. Samuel

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 1:37; Deuteronomy 4:21; Deuteronomy 9:8; Deuteronomy 9:20; 1 Kings 8:46; 1 Kings 11:9; 2 Kings 17:18; 2 Chronicles 6:36; Ezra 9:14; Psalms 2:12; Psalms 60:3; Psalms 79:5; Psalms 85:6; Isaiah 12:1


## אֲנַף (id: 000548000000000)

אֲנַף
=====

אֲנַף (A0600)

noun m

People

face \= the entire front side of a person's head, with special focus on the forehead, which is the part that touches the ground when a person does obeisance

[DAN 2:46](https://ref.ly/Dan2:46)

People

face \= the entire front side of a person's head, which reflects certain emotions, such as anger

[DAN 3:19](https://ref.ly/Dan3:19)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Daniel 2:46; Daniel 3:19


## אֲנָפָה (id: 000549000000000)

אֲנָפָה
=======

אֲנָפָה (H0601)

noun f

Birds

heron \= fairly large bird with a long neck, long beak, and long legs; they stand or walk slowly in shallow water in rivers, dams, ponds, or lakes looking into the water for small fish, which they catch by suddenly darting their heads into the water; they fly with slow wing\-beats; ► unclean and therefore not to be eaten

[LEV 11:19](https://ref.ly/Lev11:19) · [DEU 14:18](https://ref.ly/Deut14:18)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 11:19; Deuteronomy 14:18


## אנק (id: 000550000000000)

אנק
===

אנק (H0602)

verb

Sounds

to sigh, to groan, to moan \= action by which humans make a deep inarticulate sound ◄ caused by pain, grief, or severe suffering

[JER 51:52](https://ref.ly/Jer51:52) · [EZK 9:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:4) · [EZK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:17) · [EZK 26:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:15)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 51:52; Ezekiel 9:4; Ezekiel 24:17; Ezekiel 26:15


## אֲנָקָה (id: 000551000000000)

אֲנָקָה
=======

אֲנָקָה (H0603 · H0604)

noun f · אנק

Sounds

sighing, groaning, moaning \= act by which humans make a deep inarticulate sound ◄ caused by pain, grief, or severe suffering

[PSA 12:6](https://ref.ly/Ps12:6) · [PSA 79:11](https://ref.ly/Ps79:11) · [PSA 102:21](https://ref.ly/Ps102:21) · [MAL 2:13](https://ref.ly/Mal2:13)

noun f · אנק

Small Animals

gecko \= small type of lizard with pads at the end of its toes with small hairs, enabling it to walk upside down on ceilings and overhangs; only type of lizard that produces sound; feeds on insects; ◄ \|iHemidactylus turcicus\|i\*; some varieties of geckos are often found inside buildings; ► unclean animal and therefore not to be eaten

[LEV 11:30](https://ref.ly/Lev11:30)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 12:6; Psalms 79:11; Psalms 102:21; Malachi 2:13; Leviticus 11:30


## אנשׁ (id: 000552000000000)

אנשׁ
====

אנשׁ (H0605a)

verb · אִישׁ · אִישֹׁון · אֱנֹושׁ · אָנוּשׁ · אִשָּׁה

Weak

to be very ill \= state in which someone suffers from a very serious illness or disease

[2SA 12:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:15)

Evil

perverse \= state in which someone has a strong inclination towards evil

[JER 17:9](https://ref.ly/Jer17:9)

Intense

serious, grievous \= state in which someone has a very serious health condition; ► so that recovery is beyond expectation

[JOB 34:6](https://ref.ly/Job34:6) · [ISA 17:11](https://ref.ly/Isa17:11) · [MIC 1:9](https://ref.ly/Mic1:9)

Time

**Collocation:** יֹום אָנוּשׁ

day of disaster, time of disaster \= day (or: time) in which terrible events take place

[JER 17:16](https://ref.ly/Jer17:16)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Samuel 12:15; Jeremiah 17:9; Job 34:6; Isaiah 17:11; Micah 1:9; Jeremiah 17:16

Bind

to harness \= to fit an animal with a set of straps around its head ► so that one can ride it

Bind

to harness \= to fit an animal, or a pair of animals, with a set of straps or a yoke and tie it to a vehicle ► so that one can travel

Dress

to tie, to bind, to fasten \= to attach a strip of cloth to someone else's body; ≈ associated with low status

Control

prisoner, captive \= to be held by someone else, either in a prison or in another situation in which one does not have the freedom to act and go at will; ≈ usually regarded as hardship

[GEN 39:20](https://ref.ly/Gen39:20) · [GEN 40:3](https://ref.ly/Gen40:3) · [GEN 40:5](https://ref.ly/Gen40:5) · [JOB 36:8](https://ref.ly/Job36:8) · [PSA 146:7](https://ref.ly/Ps146:7) · [ECC 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:14)3 · [SNG 7:6](https://ref.ly/Song7:6) · [ISA 49:9](https://ref.ly/Isa49:9) · [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1)

Control

to capture, to imprison, to hold prisoner \= causative action by which humans hold other humans under control, either in a prison or in another situation in which that person does not have the freedom to act and go at will; ≈ regarded as hardship

Vow

## אָע (id: 000582000000000)

אָע
===

אָע (A0636)

noun m

Trees

wood \= the hard substance that makes up most of a tree ► carved or cut and assembled into objects

[DAN 5:4](https://ref.ly/Dan5:4) · [DAN 5:23](https://ref.ly/Dan5:23)

Buildings

beam \= a single piece of wood taken from a tree, cut and finished, and used for building and construction

[EZR 5:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:8) · [EZR 6:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:4) · [EZR 6:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:11)

## אַף (id: 000583000000000)

אַף
===

אַף (H0637 · A0638 · H0639)

noun m · אנף

nostrils \= the two external openings in the nose

[GEN 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7) · [GEN 7:22](https://ref.ly/Gen7:22) · [EXO 15:8](https://ref.ly/Exod15:8) · [LAM 4:20](https://ref.ly/Lam4:20)

nose \= part of the body projecting above the mouth on the face of a person or animal, containing the two nostrils

[GEN 24:47](https://ref.ly/Gen24:47) · [NUM 11:20](https://ref.ly/Num11:20) · [2SA 22:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:9) · [2SA 22:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:16) · [2KI 19:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:28) · [JOB 4:9](https://ref.ly/Job4:9)1 · [JOB 27:3](https://ref.ly/Job27:3) · [JOB 40:24](https://ref.ly/Job40:24) · [JOB 40:26](https://ref.ly/Job40:26) · [PSA 18:9](https://ref.ly/Ps18:9) · [PSA 18:16](https://ref.ly/Ps18:16) · [PSA 115:6](https://ref.ly/Ps115:6) · [PRO 11:22](https://ref.ly/Prov11:22) · [PRO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Prov30:33) · [SNG 7:5](https://ref.ly/Song7:5) · [ISA 2:22](https://ref.ly/Isa2:22) · [ISA 3:21](https://ref.ly/Isa3:21) · [ISA 37:29](https://ref.ly/Isa37:29) · [ISA 65:5](https://ref.ly/Isa65:5) · [EZK 8:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:17) · [EZK 16:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:12) · [EZK 23:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:25) · [AMO 4:10](https://ref.ly/Amos4:10)

People

face, brow \= the entire front side of a person's head, with special focus on the forehead, which is the part that touches the ground when a person does obeisance, and the part that is covered with sweat during hard work

People

face \= the entire front side of a person's head, which reflects certain attitudes, such as pride

[PSA 10:4](https://ref.ly/Ps10:4)

Space

**Collocation:** בְּאַף

before, in front of literally: in the face; hence: \= state of being located on the side of an object that presents itself to view or that is normally seen or used first

[DEU 33:10](https://ref.ly/Deut33:10)

Air

breath \= air that is expelled from the body through mouth or nose

[SNG 7:9](https://ref.ly/Song7:9)

Angry

**Collocation:** קְצַר־אַפִַּים

impatience literally: short of nostrils; hence: \= inability to restrain oneself from expressing one's displeasure; ≈ regarded as foolishness

[PRO 14:17](https://ref.ly/Prov14:17)

Patient

**Collocation:** אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם · אֹרֶךְ אַפַּיִם

patience literally: long of nostrils; hence: \= ability to restrain oneself from expressing one's displeasure; ≈ regarded as wisdom

Extenders · Affirmers

**Collocation:** אַף כִּי

how much more, how much less marker pointing to information that substantiates a particular assertion even more conclusively than previous information, which was presented in an immediately preceding utterance as an valid substantiation of that assertion

## אֵפֹוד (id: 000589000000000)

אֵפֹוד
======

אֵפֹוד (H0641 · H0646) — אֵפֹד

noun m · אפד

Clothing

**Collocation:** אֵפֹוד בָּד

linen apron, linen ephod \= garment; reaching from the shoulders to the waist; ◄ made from linen; ► normally worn by priests, but also by David when the ark was brought to Jerusalem

[1SA 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:18) · [1SA 22:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:18) · [2SA 6:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:14) · [1CH 15:27](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:27)

Clothing

ephod \= embroidered garment; reaching from the shoulders to the waist; ◄ made from linen; ► worn by the high\-priest over his robe; representing the visible glory of God; associated with divine oracles as the urim and tummim were kept in a pouch worn on top of it; this garment was regarded as unique and not to be duplicated and used outside of regulated worship

nothing \= state of being non\-existent, implying that an existing object has been destroyed, or that an object exists in people's imagination only; ≈ regarded therefore as totally insignificant

## אַקֹּו (id: 000635000000000)

אַקֹּו
======

אַקֹּו (H0689)

noun m

Wild Animals

ibex, wild goat, mountain goat \= fairly large wild goat, with adults being about ninety centimeters high at the shoulder; for most of the year its color is grayish but it turns browner in winter; males have thick long horns over 130 centimeters in length which curve backwards in a semicircle; the horns of the females are much slimmer and shorter, reaching a length of only about 40 centimeters; only the last few centimeters of the horns are smooth, the rest being ringed with ridges; these animals live in small herds in the mountains and prefer cliffs and crags, where they live off the bushes that grow on the ledges; ► clean animal, that may be eaten

[DEU 14:5](https://ref.ly/Deut14:5)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 14:5

## ארג (id: 000649000000000)

ארג
===

ארג (H0707)

verb · אֶרֶג

Weave

to weave \= causative action by which humans produce fabric by weaving

[2KI 23:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:7)

Professions

weaver \= a person who weaves fabric for a living

[EXO 28:32](https://ref.ly/Exod28:32) · [EXO 35:35](https://ref.ly/Exod35:35) · [EXO 39:22](https://ref.ly/Exod39:22) · [EXO 39:27](https://ref.ly/Exod39:27) · [1SA 17:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:7) · [2SA 21:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:19) · [1CH 11:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:23) · [1CH 20:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr20:5) · [ISA 19:9](https://ref.ly/Isa19:9) · [ISA 38:12](https://ref.ly/Isa38:12)

Weave

to weave (a spider's web) \= causative action by which spiders produce a web, which is similar in shape to woven fabric

[ISA 59:5](https://ref.ly/Isa59:5)

Attach

**Collocation:** ארג עִם

to weave (a thread) into (an existing fabric) \= causative action by which humans weave an object in the shape of a thread into an existing piece of woven fabric in a loom

[JDG 16:13](https://ref.ly/Judg16:13)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Kings 23:7; Exodus 28:32; Exodus 35:35; Exodus 39:22; Exodus 39:27; 1 Samuel 17:7; 2 Samuel 21:19; 1 Chronicles 11:23; 1 Chronicles 20:5; Isaiah 19:9; Isaiah 38:12; Isaiah 59:5; Judges 16:13

Strong

healing \= causative action by which humans or deities help or cause (other) humans to be restored to health or physical strength after a disease or an accident

[ISA 58:8](https://ref.ly/Isa58:8) · [JER 8:22](https://ref.ly/Jer8:22) · [JER 30:17](https://ref.ly/Jer30:17)

Safe

healing literally: healing; hence: \= causative action by which deities remove the consequences of evil and restore a country or nation to a state of peace and prosperity

[JER 33:6](https://ref.ly/Jer33:6)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Chronicles 24:13; Nehemiah 4:1; Isaiah 58:8; Jeremiah 8:22; Jeremiah 30:17; Jeremiah 33:6

name · רום

Names of Locations

## אֲרֹון (id: 000668000000000)

אֲרֹון
======

אֲרֹון (H0727)

noun c

Containers

box, chest, coffin \= relatively large wooden box ► used for storage

[GEN 50:26](https://ref.ly/Gen50:26) · [2KI 12:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:10) · [2KI 12:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:11) · [2CH 24:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:8) · [2CH 24:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:10) · [2CH 24:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:11) · [2CH 24:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:11)

Containers

ark \= wooden box, overlaid with gold and with a golden cover with cherubs, normally placed behind the veil in the tabernacle or temple, but also carried along during military campaigns; ► symbolizing the presence of YHWH among his people and used to store the two tablets with the law of God and other objects representing important events in the history of Israel

אֶרֶז
=====

אֶרֶז (H0730)

noun m · אַרְזָה

Trees

cedar, cedar wood \= evergreen tree growing up to 40 m tall, with a trunk up to 2\.5 m in diameter; as it grows older its branches spread out more and more horizontally; grows in elevated places; ◄ Cedrus libani; ► highly appreciated building material; also used in cleaning ritual; ≈ associated with beauty, quality, pride, and strength

ארך

verb · אָרֵךְ · אֹרֶךְ · אָרֹךְ

[1KI 8:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:8) · [2CH 5:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:9) · [EZK 31:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:5)

Long

Long

**Collocation:** ארךְ לָשֹׁון

to stick out (one's) tongue literally: to lengthen one's tongue; hence: \= to stick out one's tongue ► in order to express one's joy at someone else's misfortune

[ISA 57:4](https://ref.ly/Isa57:4)

to drive long furrows \= to make long trenches in a field with a plow, pulled by domestic animals ► in order to prepare it for cultivation

[PSA 129:3](https://ref.ly/Ps129:3)

Endure

**Collocation:** יָמִים ארךְ

days grow long \> long time passes literally: days grow long; hence: \= process in which a considerably amount of time passes

[GEN 26:8](https://ref.ly/Gen26:8) · [EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12) · [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16) · [DEU 6:2](https://ref.ly/Deut6:2) · [DEU 25:15](https://ref.ly/Deut25:15) · [EZK 12:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:22)

Endure

**Collocation:** ארךְ (יָמִים)

to prolong (days) \> to stay long, to linger, to last, to live on literally: to prolong (days); hence: \= to stay (in a certain location or position) for an extended period of time

[NUM 9:19](https://ref.ly/Num9:19) · [NUM 9:22](https://ref.ly/Num9:22) · [DEU 4:26](https://ref.ly/Deut4:26) · [DEU 4:40](https://ref.ly/Deut4:40) · [DEU 5:33](https://ref.ly/Deut5:33) · [DEU 11:9](https://ref.ly/Deut11:9) · [DEU 17:20](https://ref.ly/Deut17:20) · [DEU 22:7](https://ref.ly/Deut22:7) · [DEU 30:18](https://ref.ly/Deut30:18) · [DEU 32:47](https://ref.ly/Deut32:47) · [JOS 24:31](https://ref.ly/Josh24:31) · [JDG 2:7](https://ref.ly/Judg2:7) · [PSA 72:5](https://ref.ly/Ps72:5)1 · [PRO 28:2](https://ref.ly/Prov28:2) · [PRO 28:16](https://ref.ly/Prov28:16) · [ECC 7:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:15) · [ECC 8:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:12) · [ECC 8:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:13) · [ISA 53:10](https://ref.ly/Isa53:10)

Endure

**Collocation:** ארךְ ימִים

to prolong (someone else's) days \> to cause to live on literally: to prolong (someone else's) days; hence: \= to cause someone else to stay (in a certain location or position) for an extended period of time

[1KI 3:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:14)

Patient

**Collocation:** ארךְ אַף

to be slow to anger, to be patient literally: to prolong (\= delay) one's anger; hence: \= state in which humans or deities are able to restrain their anger

[PRO 19:11](https://ref.ly/Prov19:11) · [ISA 48:9](https://ref.ly/Isa48:9)

Patient

**Collocation:** ארךְ נֶפֶשׁ

to wait patiently literally: to prolong (\= delay) one's self; hence: \= state in which humans are able to defer action in spite of their own wish to do so

[JOB 6:11](https://ref.ly/Job6:11)

1 PSA 72:5 LXX "συμπαραμενει" NIV NJB "he will endure" RSV NRSV "may he live" CEV "let ... live" \| MT NJPS REB "fear you" GNT "worship you" \= ירא

Reinier de Blois

## אֲרַע (id: 000710000000000)

אֲרַע
=====

אֲרַע (A0772)

noun f

Land

earth \= the earth as opposed to the sky; sometimes used in a more specific sense to denote part of the earth, such as the dry land as opposed to the sea, or a specific region, area, country, or plot of land

[JER 10:11](https://ref.ly/Jer10:11) · [DAN 2:35](https://ref.ly/Dan2:35) · [DAN 2:39](https://ref.ly/Dan2:39) · [DAN 3:31](https://ref.ly/Dan3:31) · [DAN 4:7](https://ref.ly/Dan4:7) · [DAN 4:8](https://ref.ly/Dan4:8) · [DAN 4:12](https://ref.ly/Dan4:12) · [DAN 4:12](https://ref.ly/Dan4:12) · [DAN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Dan4:17) · [DAN 4:19](https://ref.ly/Dan4:19) · [DAN 4:20](https://ref.ly/Dan4:20) · [DAN 4:32](https://ref.ly/Dan4:32) · [DAN 4:32](https://ref.ly/Dan4:32) · [DAN 6:26](https://ref.ly/Dan6:26) · [DAN 7:4](https://ref.ly/Dan7:4) · [DAN 7:17](https://ref.ly/Dan7:17) · [DAN 7:23](https://ref.ly/Dan7:23) · [DAN 7:23](https://ref.ly/Dan7:23)

Insignificant

inferior literally: earth; hence: \= state in which an entity is inferior in status to other entities

[DAN 2:39](https://ref.ly/Dan2:39)

**Collocation:** שְׁמַיָּא וְאַרְעָא

heaven and earth, universe \= a more or less fixed phrase: the heavens and the earth, equivalent to a single lexical item: the totality of God's creation

[EZR 5:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:11) · [DAN 6:28](https://ref.ly/Dan6:28)

## אֶרֶץ (id: 000714000000000)

אֶרֶץ
=====

אֶרֶץ (H0776)

noun f

Land

earth, ground, land, country, region, area \= the earth as opposed to the sky; sometimes used in a more specific sense to denote part of the earth, such as the dry land as opposed to the sea, or a specific region, area, country, or plot of land

Spatial Referents

**Collocation:** כִּבְרַת־אֶרֶץ · כִּבְרַת־הָאָרֶץ

short distance literally: distance of land; hence: \= a referent pointing to a relatively short distance

[GEN 35:16](https://ref.ly/Gen35:16) · [GEN 48:7](https://ref.ly/Gen48:7) · [2KI 5:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:19)

**Collocation:** הַשָּׁמַיִם וְהָאָרֶץ

heaven and earth, universe \= a more or less fixed phrase: the heavens and the earth, equivalent to a single lexical item: the totality of God's creation

[GEN 2:4](https://ref.ly/Gen2:4) · [GEN 14:19](https://ref.ly/Gen14:19) · [GEN 14:22](https://ref.ly/Gen14:22) · [PSA 69:35](https://ref.ly/Ps69:35) · [PSA 115:15](https://ref.ly/Ps115:15) · [PSA 121:2](https://ref.ly/Ps121:2) · [PSA 124:8](https://ref.ly/Ps124:8) · [PSA 134:3](https://ref.ly/Ps134:3) · [PSA 146:6](https://ref.ly/Ps146:6) · [PSA 148:13](https://ref.ly/Ps148:13) · [JER 33:25](https://ref.ly/Jer33:25) · [JER 51:48](https://ref.ly/Jer51:48) · [JOL 4:16](https://ref.ly/Joel4:16)

## אַרְצָא (id: 000715000000000)

אַרְצָא
=======

אַרְצָא (H0777)

name

Names of People

Arza \= official; ► time of Elah, king of Israel

[1KI 16:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:9)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 16:9


## אֲרַק (id: 000716000000000)

אֲרַק
=====

אֲרַק (A0778)

noun f

**Collocation:** שְׁמַיָּא וְאַרְקָא

heaven and earth, universe \= a more or less fixed phrase: the heavens and the earth, equivalent to a single lexical item: the totality of God's creation

[JER 10:11](https://ref.ly/Jer10:11)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 10:11


## ארר (id: 000717000000000)

ארר
===

ארר (H0779) [SAHD](https://sahd-online.com/words/2-r-r/)

verb · מְאֵרָה

Curse

to curse, to place a curse \= to consign someone or something to death, damage, or destruction, usually by invoking the name of a deity if the agent is human; ◄ this is done as punishment, out of contempt, anger, or hostility, or because one was hired to do so

name

Names of Locations · Names of Groups

## ארשׂ (id: 000719000000000)

ארשׂ
====

ארשׂ (H0781)

verb

Associate

to contract in marriage, to betrothe \= action by which a man makes a marriage contract by means of the payment of a bride\-price with the family of a woman, which is usually done before the man takes the woman to live with him

## אֵשׁ (id: 000723000000000)

אֵשׁ
====

אֵשׁ (H0784 · H0800) — אֶשָּׁה

noun c · אִשֶּׁה

Fire

fire \= state of burning, in which substances combine chemically with oxygen from the air and give out bright light, heat, and smoke; ► used for cooking, melting, cleansing, heating, and destroying; ≈ many aspects of life are compared with fire, such as anger, jealousy, aggression, wickedness, words, life, certain sicknesses, suffering, etc.

אֹשׁ
====

אֹשׁ (A0787)

noun m

foundation, base \= the bottom part of a construction, which bears everything built on top of it

## אֶשָּׁא (id: 000725000000000)

אֶשָּׁא
=======

אֶשָּׁא (A0785)

noun f

Fire

fire \= state of burning, in which substances combine chemically with oxygen from the air and give out bright light, heat, and smoke; ► used for cooking, melting, cleansing, heating, and destroying; ≈ many aspects of life are compared with fire, such as anger, jealousy, aggression, wickedness, words, life, certain sicknesses, suffering, etc.

[DAN 7:11](https://ref.ly/Dan7:11)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Daniel 7:11

Names of People

Eshbaal \= man, also known as Ish\-Boshet and Ishvi; ◄ son of Saul; tribe of Benjamin; ► succeeded his father as king of the northern tribes until he was assassinated and David took his place; ≈ the author or a redactor of 2 Samuel probably changed his name to Ish\-Bosheth ("man of shame") to avoid references to the idol Baal

noun f

Landforms

slope \= side of a mountain or hill

the Slopes \= the eastern part of the hill country that slopes down towards the Dead Sea and the Jordan Valley

=========

אֵשְׁדָּת (H0799)

noun c

1 DEU 33:2 MT\-K אשׁדת \| MT\-Q אֵשׁ דָּת "fiery law" \| RSV NJPS GNT אֵשׁ דּאת (see דאה) \| NIV "mountain slopes" \= אֲשֵׁדָה

Reinier de Blois



## אִשֶּׁה (id: 000736000000000)

אִשֶּׁה
=======

אִשֶּׁה (H0801) — אִשֶּׁה

noun m · אֵשׁ

Sacrifice

offering made by fire \= action by which humans offer something to a deity by burning it partially or in its entirety on the altar; considered to have a pleasing aroma

Animals

mate \= female counterpart of a male animal

[GEN 7:2](https://ref.ly/Gen7:2) · [GEN 7:2](https://ref.ly/Gen7:2)

Classes

women \= woman as a social class, to be distinguished from the men and the children

========

אַשּׁוּר (H0804) — אַשּׁוּר

name

Names of People

Asshur \= man; ◄ son of Shem; ► regarded as founder of Assyria

Asshur, Assyria \= nation; ◄ located in Northern Mesopotamia with Nineveh as its capital; regarded as descendants of Shem

## אשׁם (id: 000751000000000)

אשׁם
====

אשׁם (H0816)

verb · אָשָׁם · אָשֵׁם · אַשְׁמָה

Guilty

to incur guilt \= state in which humans bear the responsiblity for an unlawful deed, that was committed either knowingly or unknowingly

[LEV 5:19](https://ref.ly/Lev5:19) · [LEV 5:19](https://ref.ly/Lev5:19) · [JDG 21:22](https://ref.ly/Judg21:22) · [2CH 19:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:10) · [2CH 19:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:10) · [JER 50:7](https://ref.ly/Jer50:7) · [EZK 22:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:4) · [EZK 25:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:12) · [EZK 25:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:12) · [HOS 4:15](https://ref.ly/Hos4:15) · [HOS 13:1](https://ref.ly/Hos13:1) · [HAB 1:11](https://ref.ly/Hab1:11)

Know

to realize one's guilt \= a process by which humans realize that they bear the responsiblity for an unlawful deed, that was committed either knowingly or unknowingly

[LEV 4:13](https://ref.ly/Lev4:13) · [LEV 4:22](https://ref.ly/Lev4:22) · [LEV 4:27](https://ref.ly/Lev4:27) · [LEV 5:2](https://ref.ly/Lev5:2) · [LEV 5:3](https://ref.ly/Lev5:3) · [LEV 5:4](https://ref.ly/Lev5:4) · [LEV 5:5](https://ref.ly/Lev5:5) · [LEV 5:17](https://ref.ly/Lev5:17) · [LEV 5:23](https://ref.ly/Lev5:23) · [NUM 5:6](https://ref.ly/Num5:6) · [NUM 5:7](https://ref.ly/Num5:7) · [HOS 5:15](https://ref.ly/Hos5:15)

Chastise

to bear one's guilt, to be punished \= process by which humans bear the consequences for an unlawful deed for which they are held responsible

[PSA 34:22](https://ref.ly/Ps34:22) · [PSA 34:23](https://ref.ly/Ps34:23) · [PRO 30:10](https://ref.ly/Prov30:10) · [ISA 24:6](https://ref.ly/Isa24:6) · [JER 2:3](https://ref.ly/Jer2:3) · [HOS 10:2](https://ref.ly/Hos10:2) · [HOS 14:1](https://ref.ly/Hos14:1) · [ZEC 11:5](https://ref.ly/Zech11:5)

Chastise

to make someone bear his/her guilt, to punish \= causative action by which deities make humans bear the consequences for an unlawful deed for which they are held responsible

[PSA 5:11](https://ref.ly/Ps5:11)

Chastise

to suffer \= process by which animals go through difficult circumstances; ≈ with the implication that someone else is responsible

[JOL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Joel1:18)

Impact

to bear the blame \> to be destroyed \= process by which objects are damaged and destroyed ≈ with the implication that someone else is responsible

## אַשְׁפָּה (id: 000763000000000)

אַשְׁפָּה
=========

אַשְׁפָּה (H0827) [SAHD](SAHD@http://www.sahd.div.ed.ac.uk/_media/lexeme:pdf:bgt-ashpa-williams_p-cam-1998p.pdf)

noun f

Containers · Weapons

quiver \= long, narrow container ► for storing one's arrows Possibly a case for bow and arrows, cf. SAHD article p. 5\.

[JOB 39:23](https://ref.ly/Job39:23) · [PSA 127:5](https://ref.ly/Ps127:5) · [ISA 22:6](https://ref.ly/Isa22:6) · [ISA 49:2](https://ref.ly/Isa49:2) · [JER 5:16](https://ref.ly/Jer5:16)

Weapons

**Collocation:** בְּנֵי אַשְׁפָּה

sons of the quiver \> arrows literally: sons of the quiver; hence: \= pointed missile, shot from a bow; slender and straight in shape and moving swiftly; ◄ made out of wood and/or iron; ► mainly used for hunting, in warfare, and occasionally for divination; ≈ symbol of aggression, affliction, and punishment

[LAM 3:13](https://ref.ly/Lam3:13)

P.J. Williams

* **Associated Passages:** Job 39:23; Psalms 127:5; Isaiah 22:6; Isaiah 49:2; Jeremiah 5:16; Lamentations 3:13

## אשׁר (id: 000769000000000)

אשׁר
====

אשׁר (H0833)

verb · אָשֻׁר

Act

**Collocation:** אשׁר בְּדֶרֶךְ

to follow the way (of), to act (with, as) literally: to go forward on a road; hence: \= to behave in a certain way

[PRO 4:14](https://ref.ly/Prov4:14) · [PRO 9:6](https://ref.ly/Prov9:6)

Act

to lead, to guide literally: to cause to go forward; hence: \= to provide authority and give guidance to other people

[ISA 3:12](https://ref.ly/Isa3:12) · [ISA 9:15](https://ref.ly/Isa9:15) · [ISA 9:15](https://ref.ly/Isa9:15)

Wise

**Collocation:** אשׁר לֵב בַּדֶּרֶךְ

to guide (one's heart) along the (right) road \> to think right \= literally: to cause one's heart to follow the (right) road; hence: \= to think morally and ethically correctly

[PRO 23:19](https://ref.ly/Prov23:19)

Right

to correct, to set right \= to set right an event that is wrong

[ISA 1:17](https://ref.ly/Isa1:17)

verb · אֹשֶׁר · אֶשֶׁר · אַשְׁרֵי · אָשֵׁר

Praise · Respect

to call blessed, to congratulate \= to communicate to someone else that you consider him/her or someone else fortunate and blessed by God; ≈ accompanied by an attitude of respect

[GEN 30:13](https://ref.ly/Gen30:13) · [JOB 29:11](https://ref.ly/Job29:11) · [PSA 41:3](https://ref.ly/Ps41:3) · [PSA 72:17](https://ref.ly/Ps72:17) · [PRO 3:18](https://ref.ly/Prov3:18) · [PRO 31:28](https://ref.ly/Prov31:28) · [SNG 6:9](https://ref.ly/Song6:9) · [MAL 3:12](https://ref.ly/Mal3:12) · [MAL 3:15](https://ref.ly/Mal3:15)

1 PSA 41:3 · PSA 41:3 MT\-K יאשׁר \| MT\-Q וְאֻשַּׁר

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 4:14; Proverbs 9:6; Isaiah 3:12; Isaiah 9:15; Proverbs 23:19; Isaiah 1:17; Genesis 30:13; Job 29:11; Psalms 41:3; Psalms 72:17; Proverbs 3:18; Proverbs 31:28; Song of Songs 6:9; Malachi 3:12; Malachi 3:15

because, for, in that \= state in which an event is presented as the motivation, cause, or reason for another event

Perception

so that \= a linker indicating that one proposition is the purpose or result or another

## אֶשֶׁר (id: 000771000000000)

אֶשֶׁר
======

אֶשֶׁר (H0835a)

noun m · אשׁר

Praise · Respect

happy, fortunate, blessed \= state of being considered fortunate and blessed by God; ≈ accompanied by an attitude of admiration

[PRO 29:18](https://ref.ly/Prov29:18)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 29:18


## אָשֵׁר (id: 000772000000000)

אָשֵׁר
======

אָשֵׁר (H0836)

name · אשׁר · אָשֵׁרִי

Names of People · Names of Groups · Names of Locations

Asher \= man, tribe, and territory; ◄ eighth son of Jacob and second son of Zilpah, slave of Leah; ► founder of one of the tribes of Israel

## אָשֻׁר (id: 000773000000000)

אָשֻׁר
======

אָשֻׁר (H0838) — אָשׁוּר

noun f · אשׁר

Act

step, track literally: the act or movement of putting one leg in front of the other in walking, used exclusively in a figurative way to denote part of one's journey through life; hence: \= something that is being done and, when God is the agent, something that other people ought to do as well; at the same time one can easily slip and fall, which is a metaphor for failure

[JOB 23:11](https://ref.ly/Job23:11) · [JOB 31:7](https://ref.ly/Job31:7) · [PSA 17:5](https://ref.ly/Ps17:5) · [PSA 17:11](https://ref.ly/Ps17:11)1 · [PSA 37:31](https://ref.ly/Ps37:31) · [PSA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Ps40:3) · [PSA 44:19](https://ref.ly/Ps44:19) · [PSA 73:2](https://ref.ly/Ps73:2) · [PRO 14:15](https://ref.ly/Prov14:15)

1 PSA 17:11 MT GNT "wherever I turn" \| HOTTP\-C RSV NIV NJPS NJB NRSV "they track me down" \= אִשְּׁרוּנִי \| NJB "they are advancing against me" REB "they press me hard"

prep

Identifiers

\- marker identifying the entity that is directly targeted by the event in focus, and which usually is already part of the cognitive context; described in syntactic terms as the direct object marker; untranslatable in English unless accompanied by a suffix

## אתה (id: 000795000000000)

אתה
===

אתה (H0857 · A0858)

verb · אֱלִיאָתָה

Move

to come \= to move to a certain location, seen from that location's perspective

[DEU 33:2](https://ref.ly/Deut33:2) · [DEU 33:21](https://ref.ly/Deut33:21) · [EZR 4:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:12) · [EZR 5:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:3) · [EZR 5:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:16) · [JOB 30:14](https://ref.ly/Job30:14) · [PSA 68:32](https://ref.ly/Ps68:32) · [ISA 21:12](https://ref.ly/Isa21:12) · [ISA 41:5](https://ref.ly/Isa41:5) · [ISA 41:25](https://ref.ly/Isa41:25) · [ISA 56:9](https://ref.ly/Isa56:9) · [ISA 56:12](https://ref.ly/Isa56:12) · [JER 3:22](https://ref.ly/Jer3:22) · [DAN 3:2](https://ref.ly/Dan3:2) · [DAN 3:26](https://ref.ly/Dan3:26) · [DAN 7:13](https://ref.ly/Dan7:13) · [DAN 7:22](https://ref.ly/Dan7:22)

Move

to bring \= to take something or someone to a certain location, seen from that locations's perspective

Happen

to come (upon someone), to happen (to someone) literally: to come; hence: \= process by which things happen to someone ◄ as if it were a person coming to visit

## אַתּוּן (id: 000797000000000)

אַתּוּן
=======

אַתּוּן (A0861)

noun m

Furnaces

furnace \= a construction, probably with a hole at the top and a door in its side, ◄ made of hardened clay, bricks, or metal, ► in which fuel such as charcoal, wood, or dried grass, is burned, used to burn criminals to death

אֵתָם

name

Names of Locations

Etham \= location; ◄ in eastern Egypt near Succoth and on the edge of the desert; ► Israelites encamped there on their way from Egypt to Canaan; when setting out from Etham they turned back in the direction of Pi\-Hahiroth; after having crossed the Sea of Reeds the Israelites pass through the desert of Etham
